counters.D.Va > Winston: D.Va's Fusion Cannons hit hardest exactly where Winston wants to fight, point-blank, and her 375 health plus 225 armour lets her simply out-brawl his Tesla Cannon trades. Boosters match his Jump Pack for mobility, so once he commits to a dive he can't easily disengage before Micro Missiles finish him off. Winston's Barrier Projector buys him a few seconds, but it doesn't stop D.Va from just following him in.
counters.D.Va > Widowmaker: Widowmaker's 200 health has no answer for D.Va once Boosters close the distance, and Defense Matrix can delete her scoped shots mid-flight while D.Va flies in. Once D.Va is in melee range the fight is already over, since Fusion Cannons kill far faster than Widowmaker's automatic sidearm can respond. Widowmaker's best counterplay is repositioning constantly with Grappling Hook rather than trying to trade at range.
counters.D.Va > Pharah: Pharah's whole kit is built around denying ground targets from the air, but D.Va's Boosters let her contest that same airspace and close distance instead of eating rockets from below. Defense Matrix can erase the splash damage from Rocket Launcher hits entirely, removing Pharah's main source of pressure. Once D.Va is in range, Fusion Cannons chew through Pharah's 250 health quickly.
counters.D.Va > Bastion: Bastion's Configuration: Assault deals huge sustained damage but relies on a stationary target profile, and Defense Matrix can absorb that entire stream along with his A-36 grenades. D.Va's armour lets her tank whatever gets through while Boosters close the gap he has no way to escape. Once she's on top of him, Fusion Cannons finish the job before he can reposition.
counters.Doomfist > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's 50 health and 150 shields leave almost no room for error, and he has no mobility tool to create distance once Doomfist commits. Rocket Punch or Seismic Slam closes the gap in an instant, and Power Block can absorb any Orbs of Destruction thrown on the approach. By the time Zenyatta's Orb of Harmony would matter, he's already dead.
counters.Doomfist > Ana: Ana's Sleep Dart and Biotic Rifle both need range and a clean sightline to work, but Doomfist's Rocket Punch and Seismic Slam let him close that distance almost instantly. Power Block can soak a Biotic Rifle shot on the way in, and once he's on top of her his burst outpaces her 250 health before she can reposition. Ana's counterplay is landing the dart preemptively, since once he's in melee range it's already too late.
counters.Doomfist > Widowmaker: Widowmaker depends entirely on distance to survive, and Doomfist's Rocket Punch and Seismic Slam erase that distance in a couple of seconds. Power Block lets him shrug off a scoped shot during the approach, and his burst damage kills her 200 health almost immediately once he's in melee range. She can try to disengage with Grappling Hook, but Doomfist's cooldowns usually recover faster than she can reset the angle.
counters.Doomfist > Ashe: Ashe's Viper is a mid-range precision weapon, which means she has little answer once Doomfist closes distance with Rocket Punch or Seismic Slam. Coach Gun can knock him away, but it also knocks Ashe backward into more open space rather than truly escaping, and his burst damage punishes her 250 health quickly on the follow-up. Power Block further protects him from any shots landed during the engage.
counters.Orisa > Doomfist: Fortify strips away the crowd control that makes Doomfist dangerous, so Rocket Punch's knockback and wall-slam stun simply don't stick, while also cutting the damage he can land during his all-in combo. Her 275 health plus 350 armour absorbs his burst far better than most tanks, and Energy Javelin can knock him away or stun him against a wall before he sets up his next engage. Doomfist's best play is baiting Fortify out before committing his combo.
counters.Roadhog > Doomfist: Roadhog's Chain Hook can catch Doomfist during or right after his engage, when Rocket Punch and Seismic Slam are on cooldown and he has nowhere to escape to. A hook followed by a point-blank Scrap Gun blast threatens to burst him down instantly, and Roadhog's 650 health simply absorbs whatever combo damage lands first. Take a Breather also lets him shrug off the follow-up pressure if the hook doesn't immediately kill.
counters.Domina > Reinhardt: Domina's Barrier Array is segmented, so Reinhardt's Rocket Hammer or Fire Strike can't collapse it all at once the way a single barrier would fall, letting her keep sightlines even under pressure. Sonic Repulsors knock him back and interrupt Charge before he can commit to a pin, and Crystal Charge can be lobbed over his Barrier Field to punish him for holding position. Reinhardt is left with few good options to close the distance safely.
counters.Domina > Doomfist: Sonic Repulsors punish Doomfist's all-in engage by knocking him straight back out before Rocket Punch or Seismic Slam can connect, undoing the tempo his combo depends on. Barrier Array gives Domina cover to retreat behind while he's forced to reset, and Crystal Charge's detonation punishes him for trying to push through the panels. Doomfist has to bait out Sonic Repulsors' cooldown before he can commit safely.
counters.Domina > Winston: Winston's dive plan relies on getting into Tesla Cannon range and staying there, but Sonic Repulsors knocks him straight back out the moment he closes in, resetting his Jump Pack cooldown for nothing. Barrier Array also denies him easy access, forcing him to break through segmented panels rather than a single barrier he can burst down quickly. Domina's Photon Magnum then punishes him at the range he's worst at fighting in.
counters.Ramattra > Domina: Ravenous Vortex slows and area-denies the space Domina needs to hold her poke angles, forcing her to reposition out of her own Barrier Array cover. Nemesis Form's armour lets Ramattra absorb her Photon Magnum beam while closing in with piercing punches, and Void Barrier can block her Crystal Charge from ever reaching him. Domina is forced to fight at close range, which favours Ramattra's transformed form far more than her poke kit.
counters.Hazard > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's 50 health and complete lack of mobility make him one of the easiest targets for Hazard's Violent Leap to punish, closing what should be a safe distance in an instant. Bonespur's close-range spread finishes him before Orb of Harmony's healing can matter, and Jagged Wall can cut off any retreat path. Zenyatta's only real answer is keeping himself and Orb of Discord well behind cover at all times.
counters.Hazard > Ana: Ana relies on keeping distance to land Sleep Dart and Biotic Rifle shots, but Violent Leap closes that gap before she can reliably interrupt the engage. Once Hazard is in her face, Bonespur's shotgun spread punishes her 250 health quickly, and Spike Guard makes it dangerous for her to try trading in melee range. She has to land Sleep Dart before he leaps, not after.
counters.Hazard > Widowmaker: Widowmaker's entire kit depends on maintaining range, and Violent Leap closes that distance far faster than her Grappling Hook can reset an angle. Bonespur's shotgun spread punishes her fragile 200 health immediately once he's on top of her, and Jagged Wall can cut off the escape route she'd normally use. She's forced to relocate constantly just to avoid being a walking target for his leap.
counters.Junker Queen > Reinhardt: Jagged Blade's wound damage keeps ticking regardless of Reinhardt's Barrier Field, so Junker Queen can apply pressure even while he's holding the shield up. Her Carnage cleave trades competitively with his Rocket Hammer in melee range, and Commanding Shout's temporary health lets her outlast the exchange his shield-drop trades usually favour. Rampage's wound-and-anti-heal effect is especially punishing since it undercuts whatever sustain his team is leaning on behind the barrier.
counters.Junker Queen > Zenyatta: Jagged Blade can be thrown and recalled from range to snag Zenyatta well before he expects pressure, and its wound damage keeps ticking through his Orb of Harmony healing. Junker Queen's Adrenaline Rush lets her sustain through any retaliation while closing the rest of the distance with Carnage. Zenyatta's 50 health and shields simply can't absorb a wound-and-melee combo.
counters.Junker Queen > Ana: Jagged Blade's pull can yank Ana out of position the instant she tries to create distance, and the wound it applies keeps damaging her regardless of any Biotic Grenade healing she lands. Commanding Shout's temporary health absorbs a Sleep Dart follow-up attempt, letting Junker Queen close the rest of the gap with Carnage. Ana's best counterplay is landing Sleep Dart the moment the blade is thrown, before the pull connects.
counters.Mauga > Reinhardt: Overrun is an unstoppable charge that simply barrels through Barrier Field rather than being blocked by it, putting Mauga in brawling range regardless of Reinhardt's shield uptime. His dual chainguns, one igniting and the other dealing bonus damage to burning targets, shred through Reinhardt's health pool far faster than Rocket Hammer trades back, and Cardiac Overdrive's lifesteal lets Mauga sustain through whatever Fire Strike chip damage lands. Reinhardt's Earthshatter is his best answer, but he has to land it before Overrun connects.
counters.Mauga > Roadhog: Cardiac Overdrive's damage reduction and lifesteal let Mauga win extended point-blank trades that would otherwise favour Roadhog's Scrap Gun, since Mauga's sustain scales with the very damage he's dealing back. Overrun's knockdown also punishes Roadhog for lining up a Chain Hook, disrupting his combo before it starts. In a straight brawl, Mauga simply out-sustains him.
counters.Mauga > Winston: Overrun charges straight through whatever barrier Winston tries to hide behind and knocks him down on arrival, cutting off his Jump Pack escape before he can use it. Mauga's chainguns then shred through Winston's health at close range faster than his Tesla Cannon can trade back, and Cardiac Overdrive keeps Mauga topped up throughout. Winston's only real answer is avoiding the charge lane entirely.
counters.Sigma > Mauga: Kinetic Grasp lets Sigma absorb Mauga's sustained chaingun fire directly into temporary shields, turning his primary damage source against him. Accretion's knockdown interrupts Overrun mid-charge, and Experimental Barrier can be repositioned to break his line of sight, denying the ignite-then-crit combo his chainguns rely on. Mauga has few tools to punish Sigma at the range this matchup is fought at.
counters.D.Va > Mauga: Defense Matrix can swallow Mauga's entire sustained chaingun stream, denying the damage his Berserker passive and Cardiac Overdrive sustain both depend on. D.Va's Boosters also let her disengage from an Overrun charge rather than eat the knockdown, and her armour absorbs whatever gets through anyway. Mauga is left with very little pressure against a target that can simply erase his main damage output.
counters.Orisa > Reinhardt: Fortify grants crowd-control immunity and bonus health right as Reinhardt tries to land his Charge pin, meaning the engage that usually wins him the trade does nothing. Energy Javelin can knock him back or stun him against a wall before he closes distance, and Javelin Spin destroys his Fire Strike projectiles outright. Reinhardt's shield-and-charge gameplan struggles to find an opening against her.
counters.Orisa > Roadhog: Fortify's damage reduction and bonus health blunt the burst from a hooked Scrap Gun combo, often letting Orisa survive what would kill most other tanks outright. Her 275 health plus 350 armour also outlasts prolonged Scrap Gun trades even without Fortify active, and Energy Javelin punishes Roadhog for standing still to line up his hook. Roadhog is forced to pick his hooks very carefully against her.
counters.Ramattra > Reinhardt: Void Barrier can block the lane Reinhardt needs for a Charge pin, forcing him to either wait it out or commit into a bad angle. Ravenous Vortex's slowing field denies him the mobility to reposition his own Barrier Field effectively, and Nemesis Form's armoured punches trade evenly or better against his Rocket Hammer in melee. Reinhardt's Earthshatter is his one real answer, but Ramattra can often see it coming and transform out of the knockdown window.
counters.Ramattra > Sigma: Nemesis Form's bonus armour lets Ramattra tank Sigma's Hyperspheres far better than his health pool would suggest, while Ravenous Vortex's downward pull strips away any attempt at levitating out of danger. Void Barrier also directly counters Sigma's own barrier play by controlling the same sightlines he wants. Sigma's Kinetic Grasp can absorb some of the pressure, but it doesn't stop the vortex from denying his positioning.
counters.Ramattra > Brigitte: Ravenous Vortex's slowing field punishes Brigitte the moment she tries to close in with her short-range Rocket Flail, keeping her out of Inspire range where she does the most work. Nemesis Form's armoured punches simply out-trade her flail in a straight melee exchange, and Void Barrier can block the lane she'd use for a Shield Bash engage. Brigitte is left without a safe way to apply her usual close-range pressure.
counters.Orisa > Ramattra: Fortify lets Orisa tank Ramattra's Void Accelerator fire and Nemesis Form punches without flinching, while Energy Javelin punishes him hard the moment he drops out of his armoured transformation on cooldown. Javelin Spin also destroys his projectiles outright, denying his main source of ranged pressure. Ramattra has to time his Nemesis Form very carefully to avoid getting caught during its downtime.
counters.Roadhog > Ramattra: Chain Hook can yank Ramattra straight out of the safety of his Void Barrier or before he transforms into Nemesis Form, denying the defensive tools his kit depends on. Roadhog's 650 health also shrugs off Ravenous Vortex's damage-over-time far better than most targets, letting him close the rest of the distance for a follow-up shotgun blast. A well-timed hook can kill Ramattra before his armour ever comes into play.
counters.Reinhardt > Sigma: Fire Strike is a piercing projectile that punches straight through Sigma rather than being stopped by his Experimental Barrier, chipping away at him even while he thinks he's protected. Reinhardt's Barrier Field also blocks Hyperspheres outright, letting him walk it forward and close distance for Rocket Hammer swings. Sigma's best counterplay is repositioning the barrier constantly rather than trying to hold a static angle.
counters.Reinhardt > Zarya: Reinhardt's Charge can interrupt Zarya before she's built up energy from her barriers, denying the damage scaling her entire kit revolves around. His own Barrier Field also blocks her beam so she gets no energy from poking it, and Earthshatter's knockdown is devastating against a grouped team with no barrier up to shrug it off. Zarya's only real answer is baiting Charge away before committing her barriers.
counters.Reinhardt > Ramattra: Fire Strike's piercing damage chips away at Ramattra even when he thinks Void Barrier is protecting him, and Charge threatens to interrupt Nemesis Form before it's fully set up. Reinhardt's own Barrier Field also denies Ramattra's Void Accelerator fire entirely, letting him walk forward and force a melee trade that favours his higher health pool. Ramattra has to commit his transformation early to avoid getting caught out of position.
counters.Roadhog > Winston: Chain Hook breaks the entire rhythm Winston relies on, diving in with Jump Pack, doing damage, then retreating before the ability is back up, by yanking him into point-blank Scrap Gun range at the worst possible moment. Roadhog's 650 health also means Winston's Tesla Cannon cleave simply isn't fast enough to win the trade before the hook combo finishes him. Winston's best play is avoiding Roadhog's hook line entirely rather than trying to dive through it.
counters.Roadhog > Wrecking Ball: Chain Hook can rip Wrecking Ball out of Roll form or interrupt him mid-swing on Grappling Claw, stripping away the momentum his entire kit depends on. Once hooked, a point-blank Scrap Gun blast plus a follow-up threatens to kill him even through Adaptive Shield's temporary buffer. Wrecking Ball's Piledriver is a strong answer if he can land it first, but the matchup favours whoever gets their combo off before the other.
counters.Sigma > Bastion: Kinetic Grasp lets Sigma absorb Configuration: Assault's sustained minigun fire directly into temporary shields, turning Bastion's biggest damage window against him. Experimental Barrier can also be repositioned to block his stationary Configuration: Artillery shots entirely, and Accretion's knockdown interrupts his transformation. Bastion has almost no way to punish Sigma at the range and angle control this matchup is fought at.
counters.Sigma > Widowmaker: Experimental Barrier lets Sigma deny Widowmaker's sightlines outright, forcing her to relocate constantly rather than settle into a comfortable angle. Kinetic Grasp also punishes her for poking at him directly, converting her damage into his own shields, while Hyperspheres bounce around cover to threaten her position even when she thinks she's safe. Widowmaker has very little way to fight back once he's committed to holding her lane.
counters.Sigma > Ashe: Experimental Barrier cuts off the sightlines Ashe needs for The Viper's precision shots, forcing her out of her comfort zone at mid-to-long range. Kinetic Grasp absorbs her Dynamite and rifle fire into temporary shields, and Hyperspheres' bouncing arc lets Sigma pressure her around cover she'd normally use for safety. Ashe's Coach Gun can create some separation, but it doesn't solve the sightline problem.
counters.Doomfist > Sigma: Rocket Punch and Seismic Slam close the distance before Sigma can reposition Experimental Barrier to protect himself, catching him in the exposed moment between barrier placements. Doomfist's burst combo threatens Sigma's health total quickly once engaged, since Sigma has no personal escape tool beyond Kinetic Grasp's shield conversion. Sigma's best counterplay is keeping the barrier between them at all times rather than trying to out-duel him directly.
counters.D.Va > Ashe: Defense Matrix can delete Ashe's Dynamite before it detonates and eat her rifle fire as D.Va closes in, denying her main sources of pressure. Boosters give D.Va the speed to close distance before Coach Gun can create real separation, and Micro Missiles finish the job once she's in range. Ashe is left reacting to a threat her kit isn't built to answer at close range.
counters.Sigma > Cassidy: Kinetic Grasp absorbs Peacekeeper fire directly into temporary shields, and Experimental Barrier denies Cassidy the sightlines his revolver needs to land precise shots. Sigma can also bounce Hyperspheres around cover to keep pressuring him even when he thinks he's safe behind an angle. Cassidy's Combat Roll offers some repositioning, but it doesn't solve the sightline denial.
counters.D.Va > Cassidy: Defense Matrix eats Cassidy's Flashbang and rifle fire on approach, denying the disruption he needs to control the engagement. D.Va's Boosters close distance faster than his Combat Roll can create separation, and once she's in his face, Fusion Cannons out-damage the Peacekeeper at point-blank range. Cassidy's Deadeye is his best answer, but landing it while being chased down is difficult.
counters.D.Va > Emre: Defense Matrix swallows Cyber Frag grenades and Synthetic Burst Rifle fire as D.Va closes the distance, stripping away Emre's ranged pressure before it lands. Her Boosters outpace his Siphon Blaster mobility window, and Fusion Cannons win the ensuing close-range fight easily. Emre has limited tools to disengage once she's committed to the dive.
counters.D.Va > Freja: Defense Matrix deletes Take Aim's charged shots as D.Va closes in, and her Boosters can match or outpace Freja's Quick Dash and Updraft repositioning rather than letting her simply relocate away. Once caught, Freja's 250 health falls quickly to Fusion Cannons at close range. Freja's best counterplay is using Updraft proactively rather than as a panic button.
counters.D.Va > Hanzo: Defense Matrix can delete Storm Arrows and Sonic Arrow outright, denying Hanzo the burst damage and vision his kit depends on. D.Va's Boosters close the gap on a hero with no true escape tool beyond Lunge, and Fusion Cannons finish him quickly once she's in range. Hanzo is forced to disengage entirely rather than try to out-trade her.
counters.D.Va > Sierra: Defense Matrix eats Tremor Charge's shockwave and Helix Rifle fire as D.Va closes in, and her mobility matches Sierra's Anchor Drone repositioning rather than letting her create real distance. Once caught, Fusion Cannons quickly overwhelm Sierra's 250 health at close range. Sierra's best play is keeping her drone anchored well away from D.Va's approach lanes.
counters.D.Va > Sojourn: Defense Matrix can delete Sojourn's fully charged Railgun shot entirely, denying the single biggest threat in her kit for free. D.Va's Boosters close the distance faster than Power Slide can reset an angle, and once she's in close range, Sojourn's weapon loses most of its value. Sojourn's best counterplay is disengaging the moment D.Va commits rather than trying to out-duel her.
counters.Sigma > Soldier: 76: Kinetic Grasp absorbs Soldier: 76's Heavy Pulse Rifle fire and Helix Rockets directly into temporary shields, while Experimental Barrier denies him the clean sightlines his hitscan weapon needs. Sigma can hold an angle that Soldier: 76 has no reliable way to break without exposing himself to Hyperspheres. Tactical Visor helps him land shots regardless of movement, but it doesn't solve the barrier denying line of sight in the first place.
counters.D.Va > Soldier: 76: Defense Matrix eats Helix Rockets and his sustained rifle fire as D.Va closes the gap, denying most of his damage output. Boosters close distance faster than Sprint can create separation, and once she's on top of him, Fusion Cannons win easily at close range. Soldier: 76's Biotic Field sustains him a little, but not enough to offset losing the ranged fight entirely.
counters.Sigma > Torbjörn: Experimental Barrier blocks the sightline Torbjörn's Rivet Gun and turret both need to function, letting Sigma dictate the engagement from behind cover. Kinetic Grasp also absorbs any rivets that do land into temporary shields, and Sigma's mobile positioning makes it difficult for Torbjörn to set up Deploy Turret safely in the first place. Torbjörn's Overload buff doesn't help much if he never gets a clean angle.
counters.Roadhog > Vendetta: Chain Hook interrupts Vendetta before Warding Stance is active, pulling her into point-blank Scrap Gun range where her melee-focused kit has little answer. Roadhog's 650 health also easily absorbs a Sundering Blade swing that would devastate a squishier target, and Take a Breather lets him recover from any damage that lands first. Vendetta's best counterplay is using Soaring Slice to disengage rather than trading directly.
counters.Roadhog > Venture: Venture is untargetable while burrowed, but the moment they emerge to use Clobber or fire the Smart Excavator, Chain Hook can catch them in that vulnerable window. Roadhog's 650 health also tanks Drill Dash's burst far better than most targets, letting him punish Venture hard for committing to an engage. Venture's Burrow is a strong escape, but only if it's used before the hook lands, not after.
counters.Roadhog > Kiriko: Chain Hook can catch Kiriko before she reacts with Swift Step, since the teleport requires targeting an ally rather than escaping instantly on reaction. Roadhog's hook-into-Scrap Gun combo threatens to kill her 250 health outright, and Protection Suzu only saves her if it's already active when the hook lands. Kiriko's best counterplay is staying close to allies so Swift Step is always a viable escape.
counters.Zarya > D.Va: D.Va's Fusion Cannons and Micro Missiles are exactly the kind of sustained fire Zarya wants soaking into Particle Barrier, and every hit she takes off Zarya's shields only ramps the Particle Cannon into a beam that outguns her mech's health pool. Once Zarya is charged, D.Va's mobility from Boosters doesn't change the fact her Fusion Cannons lose value at range while the beam keeps hitting. D.Va can burn Defense Matrix to blunt some return fire, but that doesn't stop the barrier from stacking energy in the first place.
counters.Zarya > Hazard: Hazard's whole game plan is closing distance with Violent Leap and holding space with Jagged Wall and Bonespur pressure, which means he's constantly feeding damage into Zarya's Particle Barrier at exactly the range where it absorbs the most. With no shield of his own, none of that energy transfer gets returned, and a stacked Zarya beam melts his health pool faster than Spike Guard can offset it. His best counterplay is holding Violent Leap until her barrier drops, then bursting her before she can reload it.
counters.Zarya > Junker Queen: Junker Queen wants extended close-range trades with Carnage and Jagged Blade, relying on Adrenaline Rush to heal off the wounds she inflicts, but every swing landed into a barriered Zarya just charges the Particle Cannon further. She has no shields to compete with, so once Zarya's beam is fully charged her wound-based sustain can't keep pace. Commanding Shout's temporary health buys her a cushion, but not enough to outlast a maxed beam.
counters.Zarya > Orisa: Orisa's Augmented Fusion Driver and Energy Javelin both deal direct damage that Zarya can bubble away with Particle Barrier or Projected Barrier, turning Orisa's own pressure into fuel for her beam. Fortify reduces what Orisa takes but does nothing to stop Zarya charging off other targets on the point, and a maxed Particle Cannon competes directly with Orisa's armour-heavy health pool. Orisa's best answer is using Javelin Spin to shred the barrier before it finishes absorbing damage.
counters.Echo > Orisa: Echo's Focusing Beam deals sharply increased damage to targets under half health, and Orisa's armour actually works against her here since it just delays rather than prevents that threshold. Echo's Flight also lets her dodge Energy Javelin and reposition above the Fusion Driver's effective angles, denying Orisa the chance to lock her down. Orisa can clear Sticky Bombs with Javelin Spin, but she has no reliable way to stop Echo diving from an angle Terra Surge can't punish in time.
counters.Bastion > Reinhardt: Configuration: Assault gives Bastion sustained damage that chews through Barrier Field far faster than most sources, and once it breaks, Reinhardt's health with no shield regeneration goes down quickly. Charge is Reinhardt's best way to close the gap and interrupt the transformation, but Bastion can reposition out of Assault mode if he sees the pin coming. Fire Strike pierces through to chip him, but it isn't fast enough to outpace the minigun's damage per second.
counters.Winston > Sigma: Winston's Jump Pack lets him leap onto Sigma before Experimental Barrier can be repositioned to block him, and the Tesla Cannon's short-range cleave punishes Sigma up close, where Hyperspheres lose their bounce-and-explode value. Sigma's Kinetic Grasp can absorb some of that pressure into shields, but Barrier Projector gives Winston a safe bubble to reset if Accretion knocks him down. Sigma has to land Accretion early to have any real answer to the dive.
counters.Winston > Widowmaker: Widow's Kiss demands range and clean sightlines to land scoped headshots, and Jump Pack closes that distance in a single leap, putting the Tesla Cannon to work at the melee range her kit is weakest at. Grappling Hook moves her to a new position but doesn't survive a follow-up leap, and low health dies fast to sustained cleave damage. Her best counterplay is using Venom Mine to reveal his approach before he closes the gap.
counters.Winston > Genji: Genji's Deflect is built to punish projectile weapons, but Winston's Tesla Cannon fires as a short-range beam/cleave rather than a reflectable projectile, so it keeps working even while Genji tries to bounce it back. Jump Pack also matches Genji's mobility, contesting the same verticality he relies on for Swift Strike, and Barrier Projector gives Winston a safe reset if the duel goes wrong. Genji's best bet is picking his engages carefully rather than trying to out-duel Winston directly.
counters.Winston > Tracer: Tracer's Pulse Pistols and Blink are lethal against most heroes, but her low health has no real answer to being cleaved by the Tesla Cannon at melee range, and Jump Pack means she can't out-position him the way she does other tanks. Barrier Projector also denies her the clean burst window Pulse Bomb needs to be effective. Her best counterplay is Recall to escape once she's low, though it doesn't change how the engagement starts.
counters.Winston > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's tiny health and shield pool make him one of the most fragile heroes in the game, and Jump Pack removes the range Zenyatta relies on to keep Orb of Discord and Orb of Destruction useful. Once Winston is on top of him, the burst window before his shields regenerate is enough for the Tesla Cannon to finish him. His best defense is landing Orb of Discord on Winston preemptively so allies can burst him down before he closes in.
counters.Bastion > Winston: Configuration: Assault's sustained minigun damage tears through Winston's health and armour faster than Barrier Projector can offset, and Bastion doesn't need to track Jump Pack precisely since the spread covers his approach angle. Winston's usual dive-and-reset pattern struggles here because retreating behind his own barrier just delays the inevitable. His best counterplay is baiting the transformation window before committing, since Bastion is far more vulnerable while stationary.
counters.Wrecking Ball > Widowmaker: Grappling Claw lets Wrecking Ball swing in from angles Widow's Kiss can't track fast enough to land a scoped shot, and Piledriver punishes her for holding a sniping position by launching her into the open. Grappling Hook repositions her, but it doesn't out-space a hamster who ignores normal sightlines entirely. Venom Mine planted near flanking paths is her best way to get early warning before he's on top of her.
counters.Wrecking Ball > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's fragile health and shields can't survive Wrecking Ball closing the distance with Grappling Claw and finishing with a Piledriver slam, and he has no mobility tool of his own to escape once Wrecking Ball commits. Orb of Discord can tag him for allies to punish, but that doesn't save Zenyatta in the moment he's isolated. His best hope is staying near cover so Adaptive Shield's engage has less room to work with.
counters.Wrecking Ball > Ana: Ana relies on line of sight and distance to land Sleep Dart and Biotic Rifle shots, but Grappling Claw ignores ground obstacles to reach her from angles she isn't watching, and Adaptive Shield means he arrives with a shield buffer already active. Once he's in her face, Biotic Grenade doesn't have the burst to save her before Piledriver connects. She can try pre-firing Sleep Dart on his approach line, but the swing speed makes that a difficult read.
counters.Wrecking Ball > Ashe: Ashe wants distance to hold angles with The Viper, but Grappling Claw closes that distance almost instantly, and Adaptive Shield means he's already tanked some of her pressure by the time he arrives. Coach Gun creates separation, but it knocks Ashe backward into more exposed positioning rather than real safety. Dynamite is her best tool here since it doesn't require precise aim to punish his predictable swing paths.
counters.Cassidy > Wrecking Ball: Flashbang can interrupt Wrecking Ball mid-swing before Piledriver connects, buying time for Peacekeeper's high single-shot damage to land clean hits while Adaptive Shield hasn't built up. Combat Roll also lets Cassidy reposition out of Grappling Claw range and reload at the same time. Wrecking Ball's best counterplay is timing his engage so Flashbang is on cooldown, since without it Cassidy's burst is what actually punishes him.
counters.Zarya > Sigma: Sigma's Hyperspheres and Accretion both deal direct damage that Zarya can bubble away with Particle Barrier, turning his own offense into fuel for a stronger Particle Cannon. Kinetic Grasp absorbs some incoming fire into shields, but it doesn't stop her barrier-based energy gain from other sources on the point. Once her beam is maxed it out-trades his shield-based health pool, so his best plan is bursting her down before she gets a barrier up.
counters.Zarya > Genji: Genji's Deflect can send Zarya's Particle Cannon shot back at her, but her barriers make her tanky enough to shrug off the counter-damage while she still charges energy from anything else he throws. Swift Strike gets him in close, but without armour or a barrier of his own, his low health doesn't survive a fully-charged beam for long. His best bet is picking fights before Zarya has energy to spend, since a stacked beam simply outclasses his damage race.
counters.Winston > Zarya: Jump Pack lets Winston leap onto Zarya before she can safely place Particle Barrier, and pressuring her immediately denies the energy stacking her whole kit depends on. The Tesla Cannon's cleave also punishes her low base health once her shields are stripped away. Zarya's best counterplay is popping her barrier the instant she sees him jump, since a barrier already up still charges her Particle Cannon even under a dive.
counters.Bastion > Zarya: Configuration: Assault's sustained fire chips through Zarya's barriers quickly, but it also feeds her Particle Cannon a large amount of absorbed energy, so a naive Bastion actually makes her more dangerous unless he finishes the shield fast. Once her shields are down, though, her modest base health has nothing left to stop the minigun. Her best play is baiting Bastion into wasting Configuration: Assault on her barrier rather than the rest of her team.
counters.Anran > Widowmaker: Inferno Rush closes distance in an instant, denying Widow's Kiss the range her kit depends on, and Dancing Blaze lets Anran dodge through a scoped shot's brief window while still dealing damage. Grappling Hook repositions Widowmaker once, but Anran's two charges of Inferno Rush mean she can chase down a single repositioning attempt. Widowmaker's best counterplay is using Venom Mine to spot Anran's approach lanes before she's already on top of her.
counters.Anran > Ana: Ana relies on keeping distance and clean sightlines for Sleep Dart and her Biotic Rifle, but Inferno Rush and Dancing Blaze let Anran close that gap unpredictably while dodging return fire. Once inside, Zhuque Fans and Fan the Flames pile on Ignition burn faster than Biotic Grenade can offset. Ana's best defense is landing Sleep Dart the instant Anran commits to her dash, since once asleep she can't fight back.
counters.Anran > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's low health and lack of any mobility ability leave him with no way to escape once Anran closes the distance with Inferno Rush, and her fire-fueled Zhuque Fans burn through his shields before Orb of Harmony can offset it. Dancing Blaze also lets her dodge through his Orb of Destruction volleys while still dealing damage. His only real counterplay is keeping Orb of Discord and allies nearby so a flank gets punished immediately.
counters.Cassidy > Anran: Flashbang interrupts Anran mid-dash before Inferno Rush or Dancing Blaze can close the gap, giving Peacekeeper's high single-shot damage a clean window. Combat Roll also lets Cassidy reposition out of her burn range and reload simultaneously, denying the sustained Ignition pressure her kit wants to build. Anran's best counterplay is baiting the Flashbang out with one dash charge before committing with the second.
counters.Ashe > Pharah: The Viper punishes Pharah's aerial angles with reliable hitscan damage that doesn't fall off with range, and Coach Gun's knockback can be angled to keep Ashe grounded and stable while shooting up. B.O.B. can also knock Pharah out of the sky when his charge lands. Pharah's Concussive Blast creates separation, but Ashe just re-levels her scope and keeps firing since she doesn't need to chase to stay effective.
counters.Ashe > Echo: Flight and Sticky Bombs want to threaten from unpredictable aerial angles, but Ashe's hitscan Viper tracks Echo through the air without the falloff that punishes slower projectile heroes, and a scoped headshot can end the engagement before Focusing Beam gets used. Dynamite also denies the ground Echo needs to land on. Echo's best counterplay is using Focusing Beam's execute the moment Ashe drops below half health, turning the fight back in her favor.
counters.Ashe > Mercy: Guardian Angel keeps Mercy mobile between allies, but Ashe's precise Viper can pick her off mid-flight or the instant she's tethered to a target, and Dynamite denies the safe pocket she likes to heal from. B.O.B.'s knock-up also disrupts her positioning entirely. Mercy's only real answer is staying attached to a frontline ally so Guardian Angel always has an escape route away from Ashe's sightline.
counters.Ashe > Widowmaker: Coach Gun lets Ashe create instant separation if Widowmaker lines up a scoped shot, and Dynamite can force her out of a sniping perch without needing precise aim. In direct trades, Ashe's rate of fire and mobility give her the edge over Widowmaker's slower bolt-action rhythm at anything other than maximum range. Widowmaker's best counterplay is using Venom Mine to control the angle before Ashe can close in.
counters.Winston > Ashe: Ashe wants distance and clean sightlines for The Viper, but Jump Pack erases that distance instantly, and Barrier Projector gives Winston cover to reposition if Coach Gun knocks him back. Once he's in her face, Ashe's low mobility means she can't disengage the way she can from slower threats. Her best counterplay is landing Dynamite as Winston lands, since the burn damage punishes him for closing in.
counters.Genji > Ashe: Deflect can send The Viper's shots and Dynamite right back at Ashe, turning her own damage against her, while Swift Strike lets Genji close in faster than Coach Gun's knockback can create real separation. His double jump and wall climb also let him take angles her hitscan rifle has to track precisely. Ashe's best counterplay is landing B.O.B. to knock Genji out of his dash rhythm and buy space to reset.
counters.Bastion > Mauga: Configuration: Assault's sustained damage output actually out-races Mauga's chainguns in a straight duel, since Mauga has to land criticals on burning targets to ramp up while Bastion's minigun is already at full output. Cardiac Overdrive offsets some of that with lifesteal, but Bastion's raw DPS advantage plus A-36 Tactical Grenade chip damage usually wins the sustained trade. Mauga's best counterplay is using Overrun to close the gap and knock Bastion out of his stationary firing lines.
counters.Bastion > Roadhog: Chain Hook wants to pull Bastion into range for a follow-up Scrap Gun blast, but Configuration: Assault's damage output punishes Roadhog for standing still to line up the hook, and Take a Breather can't out-heal the sustained minigun fire. A-36 Tactical Grenade also chips Roadhog down before he even commits. His best counterplay is hooking from an unexpected angle so Bastion can't react before the follow-up shot lands.
counters.Genji > Bastion: Bastion loses most of his mobility while locked into Configuration: Assault, and Swift Strike lets Genji close the gap and disrupt the transformation before it does real damage, while Deflect sends A-36 Tactical Grenade and Configuration: Recon fire straight back at him. His wall climb and double jump also let him flank from angles Bastion's forward-facing minigun can't track. Bastion's best counterplay is retreating into Configuration: Recon's mobility the instant Genji closes in.
counters.Cassidy > Tracer: Tracer's Blink and high mobility usually let her dictate engagements, but Flashbang can stun her mid-combo, and once disrupted, Peacekeeper's high single-shot damage kills her low health almost instantly. Combat Roll also lets Cassidy reload and reposition fast enough to keep pace with her hit-and-run pattern. Tracer's best counterplay is baiting the Flashbang out with a Blink charge before committing to her real engage.
counters.Cassidy > Genji: Genji's low health and lack of a barrier mean Peacekeeper can burst him down quickly once Flashbang disrupts a Deflect or Swift Strike attempt. Deflect can reflect Peacekeeper's shots, but Cassidy simply waits it out or repositions with Combat Roll rather than feeding into it. Genji's best counterplay is timing Swift Strike's dash through Cassidy the instant Flashbang is on cooldown.
counters.Cassidy > Sombra: Stealth and Translocator want to set up a clean flank, but Flashbang can stun Sombra the moment Hack ends, and Peacekeeper's burst damage punishes her low health before Virus can finish its damage-over-time tick. Combat Roll also lets Cassidy escape a Hack attempt mid-cast. Sombra's best counterplay is using Translocator to disengage the instant the Flashbang lands rather than trying to fight through the stun.
counters.Cassidy > Pharah: Pharah's aerial angles are usually safe from ground-based damage, but Peacekeeper is hitscan and doesn't fall off at range, letting Cassidy land precise shots the instant she's exposed mid-air. Flashbang can also stun her out of a Jump Jet, denying the safety her hover passive normally provides. Pharah's best counterplay is using Concussive Blast to knock Cassidy away before he lines up a clean shot.
counters.Winston > Cassidy: Cassidy relies on precise aim with Peacekeeper at range, but Jump Pack closes that gap in an instant, and the Tesla Cannon's cleave doesn't require the same accuracy he needs to fight back. Combat Roll creates some separation, but Winston's own mobility means he can simply leap again. Cassidy's best counterplay is landing Flashbang the moment Winston commits to the jump, buying enough time to kill him or escape.
counters.Echo > Pharah: Pharah's Barrage window and general burst rely on being at range, but Focusing Beam deals dramatically increased damage to targets below half health, punishing her hard once Tri-Shot chips her down. Flight also lets Echo match Pharah's aerial mobility instead of fighting from the ground. Pharah's best counterplay is using Concussive Blast to knock Echo away before the execute threshold is reached.
counters.Echo > Mercy: Guardian Angel keeps Mercy mobile, but Flight lets Echo chase through the air just as easily, and once Mercy drops below half health, Focusing Beam's execute damage finishes her before Flash Heal can save her. Sticky Bombs also punish her for staying near a single ally too long. Mercy's best counterplay is staying attached to a frontline tank so Guardian Angel always has a quick escape route.
counters.Echo > Bastion: Configuration: Assault demands Bastion plant himself in place, which gives Tri-Shot and Sticky Bombs a stationary target to chip down, and once he's below half health, Focusing Beam ends the fight fast. Flight also lets Echo approach from an angle his forward-facing minigun can't track. Bastion's best counterplay is dropping out of Configuration: Assault the instant he's flanked rather than trying to out-damage her in place.
counters.Echo > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's fragile health and shields fall well within Focusing Beam's execute range almost immediately after a Tri-Shot burst, and he has no mobility ability to escape once Flight closes the distance. Orb of Discord can tag her for team follow-up, but that doesn't save him in the moment he's isolated. His best defense is keeping allies close enough to punish Echo's dive immediately.
counters.Cassidy > Echo: Flight makes Echo a moving target, but Peacekeeper is precise hitscan that doesn't need to lead much, and Flashbang can stun her out of the air before Focusing Beam or Duplicate gets used. Combat Roll also gives Cassidy the mobility to keep pace with her repositioning. Echo's best counterplay is using Sticky Bombs from range rather than committing to Flight until Cassidy's Flashbang is on cooldown.
counters.Emre > Soldier: 76: Soldier: 76's Heavy Pulse Rifle wants sustained mid-range trades, but Emre's Synthetic Burst Rifle and Cyber Frag punish him in exactly that range, and Siphon Blaster's mobility lets Emre reposition faster than Sprint alone can create separation. Biotic Field offers some sustain, but it doesn't outpace Emre's burst damage in a straight fight. Soldier's best counterplay is using Tactical Visor to guarantee hits before Emre can fully commit to the trade.
counters.Emre > Reaper: Reaper wants to close to point-blank range where Hellfire Shotguns do the most damage, but Cyber Frag and burst rifle fire punish him during the approach, and Siphon Blaster gives Emre the mobility to keep distance rather than get caught in Reaper's ideal range. Wraith Form lets Reaper close the gap safely, but once forced to fight at range instead of point-blank, his shotguns lose most of their value. His best counterplay is using Shadow Step to flank rather than approaching head-on.
counters.Emre > Bastion: Configuration: Assault punishes anyone caught in the open, but Cyber Frag can pressure Bastion from around corners where line of sight breaks the minigun's effectiveness, and Siphon Blaster lets Emre reposition out of his firing lane quickly. Emre's burst rifle also chips away at Bastion's health between transformations. Bastion's best counterplay is repositioning to a firing angle before Emre can use cover against him.
counters.Ashe > Emre: Emre's Synthetic Burst Rifle wants mid-range fights, but Ashe's Viper out-ranges him with scoped precision shots that punish him before Cyber Frag's grenades reach effective distance. Dynamite also denies the corners Emre likes to use for burst pressure. Emre's best counterplay is using Siphon Blaster's mobility to close the gap where his burst rifle actually competes.
counters.Freja > Pharah: Updraft lets Freja contest the same vertical space Pharah relies on for safety, and Take Aim can pick her out of the air with precision that matches Pharah's own aerial threat. Quick Dash also gives Freja the mobility to reposition mid-air fight rather than being stuck on the ground. Pharah's best counterplay is using Concussive Blast to knock Freja away before she lines up a clean shot.
counters.Freja > Echo: Flight wants to threaten from unpredictable angles, but Updraft and Quick Dash let Freja match that verticality and mobility, and Take Aim punishes Echo the moment she's airborne and exposed. Sticky Bombs need a stationary target to really land, which Freja's dash denies her. Echo's best counterplay is using Focusing Beam's execute the instant Freja drops below half health.
counters.Freja > Mercy: Guardian Angel keeps Mercy safely tethered to allies, but Updraft and Quick Dash let Freja take unconventional angles that pressure her even mid-flight, and a clean Take Aim shot threatens Mercy's fragile health before Flash Heal can offset it. Mercy's best counterplay is staying close to a frontline ally so Guardian Angel always has an immediate retreat path away from Freja's sightline.
counters.Winston > Freja: Freja relies on Quick Dash and Updraft to control range and pick her shots, but Jump Pack removes that range advantage in a single leap, and the Tesla Cannon's cleave doesn't require precision to punish her once he's close. Barrier Projector also gives Winston cover to reset if she creates separation. Freja's best counterplay is using Updraft immediately to escape to high ground the moment he closes in.
counters.Genji > Widowmaker: Deflect can send Widow's Kiss shots right back at her, and Swift Strike's dash closes the range she depends on almost instantly. Genji's wall climb also lets him reach whatever perch she's using rather than approaching from a predictable angle. Widowmaker's best counterplay is using Venom Mine to spot his approach and Grappling Hook to reposition before he's in melee range.
counters.Genji > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's low health has essentially no answer to a Swift Strike dash followed by Dragonblade, and he lacks any mobility tool to disengage once Genji has committed. Deflect also nullifies Orb of Destruction's damage output entirely. His best defense is keeping Orb of Discord ready to punish Genji's dive with team follow-up before the duel even starts.
counters.Genji > Ana: Ana wants to land Sleep Dart from range to stop a dive before it starts, but Swift Strike closes that distance almost instantly, and Deflect can turn her Biotic Rifle shots back at her if she tries to peck at him from afar. His double jump and wall climb also let him approach from angles she isn't watching. Ana's best counterplay is pre-firing Sleep Dart on his likely dash lane rather than waiting to react.
counters.Winston > Hanzo: Hanzo wants distance to fully draw Storm Bow shots, but Jump Pack erases that range instantly, and the Tesla Cannon's cleave punishes his low mobility once cornered. Lunge creates some separation, but it's a single dash against a hero who can simply jump again. Hanzo's best counterplay is using Sonic Arrow to track Winston's approach before he commits to an engage.
counters.Genji > Hanzo: Deflect can send Storm Arrows and charged shots straight back at Hanzo, and Swift Strike closes the gap before he can fully draw a lethal shot. Genji's wall climb also lets him take the same angles Hanzo relies on, denying him a safe perch. Hanzo's best counterplay is using Sonic Arrow to track Genji's position and land a shot before he closes in.
counters.Echo > Junkrat: Junkrat's Frag Launcher and Steel Trap want to control chokepoints and ground-level space, but Flight lets Echo bypass all of that entirely, and Focusing Beam punishes him hard once his health drops below half from Tri-Shot pressure. Concussion Mine has some vertical reach, but it's not built to reliably track an airborne target. Junkrat's best counterplay is using RIP-Tire's wall-climbing to threaten Echo from angles she isn't expecting.
counters.Zarya > Junkrat: Junkrat's explosive spam is exactly the kind of direct damage Zarya's barriers are built to absorb, converting Frag Launcher and Concussion Mine hits straight into Particle Cannon energy. Once she's charged, her beam kills his low health faster than his own burst can answer back, since he has no shield of his own to trade against. His best counterplay is targeting her allies instead of feeding a barriered Zarya more energy.
counters.Echo > Mei: Ice Wall and Cryo-Freeze are built to control ground-level engagements, but Flight lets Echo simply go over the wall and attack from angles the short-range Endothermic Blaster can't easily track. Focusing Beam also punishes Mei hard once her health dips below half. Mei's best counterplay is using Ice Wall defensively to block Echo's flight path rather than trying to split the fight.
counters.Zarya > Mei: Endothermic Blaster and Ice Wall want to control close-range engagements, but every hit landed on a barriered Zarya just charges the Particle Cannon further, and Cryo-Freeze can delay the inevitable but not out-heal a fully charged beam. Mei has no shield of her own to compete with Zarya's stacked energy output. Her best counterplay is using Ice Wall to physically separate herself from Zarya rather than trading damage directly.
counters.Echo > Reaper: Reaper wants to close into shotgun range, but Flight lets Echo stay airborne and out of Hellfire Shotguns' effective distance while Tri-Shot chips him down. Once he drops below half health, Focusing Beam finishes him before Wraith Form's invulnerability window can reliably save him. Reaper's best counterplay is using Shadow Step to flank from an angle where Echo isn't watching, rather than approaching head-on.
counters.Cassidy > Reaper: Hellfire Shotguns need point-blank range to be effective, but Peacekeeper punishes Reaper from a distance he can't close fast enough, and Flashbang can stun him mid-approach before Wraith Form saves him. Combat Roll also lets Cassidy create separation and reload simultaneously. Reaper's best counterplay is using Shadow Step to bypass the range disadvantage entirely and flank instead.
counters.Cassidy > Shion: Shion wants to duel at close to mid-range with her Kira Pistols, but Flashbang can stun her out of an Evade dash, and Peacekeeper's high single-shot damage wins a straight trade against her flanker health pool. Combat Roll also keeps Cassidy's ammo topped up during the exchange. Shion's best counterplay is using Joyride to disengage rather than trying to out-duel him directly.
counters.Ashe > Sierra: Helix Rifle becomes more accurate the longer Sierra sustains fire, but Ashe's Viper punishes her before that ramp-up pays off, and Dynamite denies the corners Anchor Drone likes to use for repositioning. Coach Gun also creates separation if Sierra tries to close in with Tremor Charge. Her best counterplay is using Tracking Shot to mark Ashe early and coordinate burst before she gets a clean scoped shot off.
counters.Winston > Sojourn: Sojourn needs sustained primary fire to charge her Railgun into a lethal secondary shot, but Jump Pack closes the distance before she can fully build that charge, and the Tesla Cannon's cleave punishes her at the range her Railgun is least effective. Power Slide creates some separation, but Winston simply jumps again. Sojourn's best counterplay is using Disruptor Shot to slow him before he reaches melee range.
counters.Genji > Sojourn: Deflect can send Sojourn's charged Railgun shot straight back at her, denying the exact payoff her whole kit builds toward, and Swift Strike closes the distance before she can safely disengage with Power Slide. Sojourn's best counterplay is using Disruptor Shot to slow Genji's approach and buy time to reposition before Deflect comes up again.
counters.Genji > Soldier: 76: Heavy Pulse Rifle relies on sustained tracking damage, but Deflect turns that fire back at Soldier: 76, and Swift Strike closes the gap before Tactical Visor's auto-aim window can be used safely. Biotic Field offers some sustain, but it doesn't outlast a Dragonblade combo. His best counterplay is using Helix Rockets to punish Genji before he can commit to Swift Strike.
counters.Echo > Symmetra: Symmetra's low health and shield pool make her extremely vulnerable to Focusing Beam once Tri-Shot brings her below half health, and her Sentry Turrets don't track a flying target well. Photon Barrier can block incoming fire but doesn't stop Echo from simply flying around it. Symmetra's best counterplay is using her Teleporter to reposition away from the dive before it fully commits.
counters.Winston > Symmetra: Symmetra's low health pool can't survive the Tesla Cannon's cleave once Jump Pack closes the distance, and her Sentry Turrets are built for area denial rather than stopping a direct dive. Photon Projector needs sustained beam contact to ramp up, which a mobile Winston denies her. Her best counterplay is placing turrets to cover her own position before Winston can leap in unnoticed.
counters.Echo > Torbjörn: Deploy Turret controls ground-level chokepoints, but Flight lets Echo bypass the turret's field of fire entirely and pressure Torbjörn directly with Tri-Shot and Focusing Beam. Overload helps him trade in a straight fight, but it doesn't give him the mobility to track an airborne threat. His best counterplay is anchoring his turret somewhere with vertical coverage to threaten Echo's approach.
counters.Echo > Vendetta: Vendetta's kit is built around close-range sword duels with Whirlwind Dash and Soaring Slice, but Flight lets Echo simply stay out of melee range while Tri-Shot and Sticky Bombs whittle her down. Once Vendetta drops below half health, Focusing Beam finishes the job before Warding Stance can block enough damage to matter. Her best counterplay is using Projected Edge to threaten Echo from range instead of committing to a melee approach.
counters.Echo > Venture: Burrow makes Venture briefly untargetable, but it doesn't let them threaten an airborne Echo, and Drill Dash's short range means they have to commit to ground-level engagements exactly where Flight lets Echo disengage. Focusing Beam also punishes Venture hard once Smart Excavator pressure brings them below half health. Their best counterplay is using Clobber's enhanced melee the instant Echo lands rather than chasing her in the air.
counters.Cassidy > Venture: Drill Dash and Burrow want to close distance unpredictably, but Flashbang can stun Venture the instant they emerge, and Peacekeeper's burst damage punishes their exposed frame before Clobber's melee ever lands. Combat Roll also lets Cassidy reposition away from the burrow's emergence point. Venture's best counterplay is using Burrow to reposition mid-fight rather than committing to a single predictable dash.
counters.Winston > Ana: Ana wants distance to safely land Sleep Dart and Biotic Rifle shots, but Jump Pack closes that gap instantly, and Barrier Projector gives Winston cover to survive the window before Biotic Grenade's anti-heal matters. Once he's on top of her, Ana's low mobility means she can't disengage easily. Her best counterplay is landing Sleep Dart the moment she sees him commit to the jump, since a slept Winston can't follow up.
counters.Genji > Baptiste: Immortality Field can save Baptiste from a finishing blow, but Deflect nullifies his Biotic Launcher's burst damage entirely, and Swift Strike lets Genji close the distance before Baptiste can reposition with Exo Boots. Regenerative Burst offers some sustain, but it doesn't out-heal a full Dragonblade combo. His best counterplay is using Immortality Field proactively to survive the initial engage rather than reacting to it.
counters.Winston > Baptiste: Biotic Launcher wants mid-range engagements, but Jump Pack removes that range instantly, and the Tesla Cannon's cleave punishes Baptiste up close where his burst damage is less useful than his healing kit. Immortality Field prevents him from dying outright, but it doesn't stop Winston from picking off other targets while it's active. His best counterplay is using Exo Boots to gain height and create separation before Winston lands.
counters.Echo > Brigitte: Barrier Shield and Shield Bash are built for close-range peel, but Flight lets Echo simply stay in the air where Rocket Flail can't reach, and Focusing Beam punishes Brigitte hard once she's below half health. Repair Pack sustains allies, but it doesn't help Brigitte survive a direct dive from above. Her best counterplay is using Whip Shot's ranged knockback to interrupt Echo's approach before she gets close enough to threaten.
counters.Winston > Illari: Solar Rifle wants range to land precise scoped shots, but Jump Pack closes that gap instantly, and Healing Pylon doesn't provide the burst healing needed to offset the Tesla Cannon's sustained cleave. Outburst creates some separation, but it's a single knockback against a hero who can simply jump again. Illari's best counterplay is using Outburst proactively the moment Winston lands to buy space before he can follow up.
counters.Genji > Illari: Solar Rifle needs precise aim at range, but Swift Strike closes that gap before Illari can land a clean scoped shot, and Deflect sends her damage right back at her if she tries to fight at a distance. Healing Pylon offers passive sustain, but it doesn't save her from a direct Dragonblade combo. Her best counterplay is using Outburst's knockback the instant Genji dashes in.
counters.Ashe > Jetpack Cat: Permanent flight keeps Jetpack Cat airborne, but Ashe's hitscan Viper tracks aerial targets without the falloff that punishes slower projectile weapons, and Dynamite denies the safe hovering positions they rely on. B.O.B. also disrupts their flight path entirely when he lands. Their best counterplay is using Frenetic Flight to break line of sight rather than staying in a predictable hover.
counters.Genji > Juno: Glide Boost keeps Juno mobile in the air, but Genji's double jump and wall climb let him match that verticality, and Swift Strike closes the remaining gap for a Dragonblade finish. Hyper Ring helps her escape along a set path, but that doesn't help if he's already on top of her. Her best counterplay is using Pulsar Torpedoes at range before he can close in, since the Mediblaster falls off in close combat anyway.
counters.Winston > Juno: The Mediblaster loses effectiveness beyond 25 metres, and Jump Pack forces exactly the close-range engagement where Glide Boost mobility stops mattering. The Tesla Cannon's cleave then punishes Juno's fragile health and shields quickly. Her best counterplay is using Hyper Ring to create separation and reposition to a safer range before Winston lands again.
counters.Cassidy > Kiriko: Swift Step and Protection Suzu give Kiriko strong defensive tools, but Flashbang can stun her before she reacts, and Peacekeeper's burst damage punishes her once she's disrupted. Combat Roll also lets Cassidy dodge her Kunai throws while repositioning. Her best counterplay is using Protection Suzu proactively to cleanse the stun and grant brief invulnerability the moment Flashbang lands.
counters.Genji > Lifeweaver: Life Grip can pull an ally to safety, but it doesn't save Lifeweaver himself from Swift Strike, and Deflect nullifies Thorn Volley's damage output entirely. Petal Platform offers an escape route, but Genji's double jump and wall climb let him follow just as easily. His best counterplay is using Rejuvenating Dash to create separation the instant Genji closes in.
counters.Winston > Lifeweaver: Healing Blossom needs a charge-up window to land full value, but Jump Pack interrupts that window by forcing Lifeweaver to react instead, and the Tesla Cannon punishes his low mobility once cornered. Petal Platform can launch him to safety, but Winston can simply follow with another jump. His best counterplay is using Rejuvenating Dash to escape toward a frontline ally, since Life Grip only pulls others to him rather than himself to safety.
counters.Ashe > Lúcio: Wall Ride and Crossfade keep Lúcio mobile and healing, but Ashe's precise Viper can pick him off the wall with a well-placed shot, and Dynamite denies the corners he uses to break line of sight. Soundwave can knock Ashe away, but her Coach Gun answers with the same tool in reverse. Lúcio's best counterplay is switching to Speed Song to close distance and disrupt Ashe's sightline before she lines up a shot.
counters.Cassidy > Lúcio: Wall Ride and constant repositioning keep Lúcio safe from most threats, but Peacekeeper punishes clean hits regardless of his speed, and Flashbang can stun him mid-ride to guarantee the follow-up shot. Amp It Up doesn't provide the burst healing needed to out-sustain that combo. His best counterplay is using Soundwave to knock Cassidy away before Flashbang lands.
counters.Genji > Mercy: Guardian Angel keeps Mercy attached to allies, but Swift Strike dashes through those same allies to reach her directly, and Deflect denies her Caduceus Blaster any value as self-defense. Resurrect can bring back a fallen ally, but it doesn't save Mercy from a direct Dragonblade combo. Her best counterplay is staying close to a frontline tank so Guardian Angel always has an immediate escape path away from Genji's dash lane.
counters.Winston > Mercy: Guardian Angel gives Mercy mobility between allies, but Jump Pack matches that mobility and lets Winston follow her tether directly, and the Tesla Cannon punishes her fragile health quickly once cornered. Her Caduceus Blaster isn't built for serious self-defense. Her best counterplay is using Guardian Angel toward a frontline ally the instant Winston commits, rather than trying to outrun him solo.
counters.Echo > Moira: Fade grants brief invulnerability, but it's on a cooldown, and Focusing Beam punishes Moira hard the instant her health dips below half from Tri-Shot pressure. Biotic Orb offers some poke, but it doesn't reliably track an airborne Echo. Her best counterplay is saving Fade specifically for the moment Echo commits to a dive rather than using it early.
counters.Genji > Wuyang: Restorative Stream and Guardian Wave want sustained positioning to keep healing flowing, but Swift Strike closes the distance before Rushing Torrent can create real separation, and Deflect turns his Xuanwu Staff shots back at him. His best counterplay is using Tidal Blast's overhealth barrier proactively to survive the initial burst before Dragonblade follows up.
counters.Symmetra > D.Va: Symmetra's Photon Projector ramps up the longer it stays locked onto one target, and D.Va's close-range Fusion Cannons force her into exactly the sustained contact that lets the beam reach full damage. Defense Matrix stops incoming projectiles but does nothing to prevent the ramp from building, and Sentry Turrets add chip damage from angles Boosters can't easily clear. D.Va's best counterplay is to dip in and out with Boosters rather than sit in beam range long enough for it to ramp.
counters.Mei > D.Va: Mei's Endothermic Blaster slows on contact, stripping away the mobility edge Boosters normally gives D.Va, and once she's snared long enough Mei can freeze her solid for a free follow-up kill. Ice Wall can also cut D.Va off from her team mid-dive, isolating her well before Self-Destruct becomes a threat. D.Va's counterplay is to disengage with Boosters at the very first sign of the slow rather than trying to push through it.
counters.Sombra > D.Va: Hack strips away Defense Matrix and Boosters at the exact moment D.Va needs them to dive safely or bail out, leaving her as little more than a slow-firing mech. Once hacked, Sombra's Machine Pistol finishes the job at close range while Translocator lets her pick the moment to strike. D.Va's counterplay is to commit to a dive only when Sombra's Hack is visibly on cooldown.
counters.Sombra > Doomfist: Doomfist's entire game plan is chaining Rocket Punch into Seismic Slam to burst a target down before they can react, but Hack disables whichever ability he needs mid-combo and denies the overhealth his passive grants from ability damage. Stealth also lets Sombra avoid ever being the first target he sees. He can bait the Hack out with Power Block before committing, but that telegraphs the engage and costs him the tempo his kit depends on.
counters.Sombra > Domina: Domina's Reconstruction passive only refills her shields when she lands ability damage, so Hack denies Barrier Array or Crystal Charge at the moment she needs them to stay topped up. Without a segmented barrier to duck behind, her thin 100 health is exposed, and Translocator lets Sombra dive past Sonic Repulsors' knockback to keep the pressure on. Domina's counterplay is to hold a barrier panel in reserve and raise it the instant she's hacked.
counters.Reaper > Domina: Reaper's Hellfire Shotguns tear through Domina's slim 100 health once her Barrier Array panels are broken, and Wraith Form lets him walk straight through Sonic Repulsors and Crystal Charge without taking damage on the approach. Shadow Step also lets him bypass her barrier entirely and appear at flanking range where a single panel can't cover him. Domina's best answer is to keep a barrier segment between herself and any angle Reaper might take.
counters.Mei > Hazard: Hazard wants to close distance with Violent Leap and hold ground with Jagged Wall, but Mei's slowing beam blunts that approach and her Ice Wall can block his leap path outright, denying the engage before it starts. Once slowed, a freeze turns his brawl attempt into a free kill for her team. Hazard's counterplay is to bait the slow out from behind Spike Guard before committing to the leap.
counters.Reaper > Hazard: Hazard's 475 health and 225 armor look imposing, but Reaper's Hellfire Shotguns shred armor at point-blank range faster than Bonespur can trade back, especially once Reaper closes the gap with Shadow Step or survives the approach in Wraith Form. The lifesteal from The Reaping also out-sustains anything Spike Guard can return. Hazard needs to use Jagged Wall to cut off the flank before Reaper ever reaches shotgun range.
counters.Mei > Junker Queen: Junker Queen wants to be in melee range stacking wounds with Carnage and Jagged Blade, but Mei can wall off her charge in with Ice Wall or slow her with the Endothermic Blaster to keep the fight at a range Scattergun can't punish. Once slowed, a freeze shuts her down completely, denying Adrenaline Rush the damage it needs to heal her. Her only recourse is closing the gap fast, which Commanding Shout's speed boost helps with.
counters.Symmetra > Orisa: Symmetra's charged orbs and ramping beam chew through Orisa's 350 armor efficiently since sustained contact keeps stacking damage rather than resetting, and Sentry Turrets add unavoidable chip damage on top. Photon Barrier can also completely block the payload push that Fortify and Terra Surge are built to enable. Orisa's counterplay is to use Javelin Spin to destroy the turrets and clear a path forward.
counters.Pharah > Reinhardt: Reinhardt's whole defensive toolkit — Barrier Field, Charge, Fire Strike — is grounded, and none of it reaches Pharah once she's using Jump Jet to hover above the barrier's coverage. Her Rocket Launcher's splash punishes him for holding still, and Barrage can finish him from an angle Barrier Field simply can't block. Reinhardt's best chance is baiting her into Concussive Blast range with a well-timed Fire Strike.
counters.Sombra > Sigma: Kinetic Grasp only absorbs projectiles, so it offers no protection against Hack, which shuts down Experimental Barrier or Accretion right when Sigma needs them to control space. Once hacked, his 0-armor 350 health has nothing to hide behind, and Sombra's Virus finishes what the Machine Pistol started. Sigma can bait the Hack onto a wasted Kinetic Grasp activation before committing to an engage.
counters.Reaper > Winston: Winston's Tesla Cannon is a short-range cleave meant for mobile targets, but Reaper's Wraith Form lets him walk straight through a Jump Pack engage without taking damage, then unload Hellfire Shotguns at exactly the range Winston wanted to fight at. Barrier Projector can't stop Reaper once Shadow Step has already put him inside it. Winston's best chance is disengaging with a second Jump Pack rather than trading shots.
counters.Sombra > Wrecking Ball: Wrecking Ball's Adaptive Shield scales with nearby enemies, but Hack interrupts Roll or Grappling Claw mid-swing, stranding him in the open where his momentum-based kit becomes useless. Once hacked, the Machine Pistol punishes his lack of armor before Piledriver can bail him out. His counterplay is to stay in constant motion so Hack's cast time can't catch him mid-swing.
counters.Mei > Wrecking Ball: Mei's Ice Wall can cut off Wrecking Ball's momentum-based swing paths entirely, and her slow saps the speed that Grappling Claw and Roll depend on to be dangerous. Once he's caught out of ball form or slowed, a freeze locks him down for a full team punish. His counterplay is to build speed on an open lane before Mei can predict and wall it off.
counters.Pharah > Zarya: Zarya's barriers only stop damage aimed at herself or an ally on the ground, but Pharah's Barrage comes from angles those barriers were never raised to cover, and her Rocket Launcher's splash lets her chip Zarya from beyond effective beam range. Particle Cannon falls off badly at the distance Pharah maintains with Jump Jet. Zarya's best answer is baiting Pharah down to ground level where her beam's damage scaling actually matters.
counters.Mei > Anran: Anran's Inferno Rush and Dancing Blaze are built around darting into range and back out, but Mei's slow neutralizes the burst mobility those abilities rely on, and Ice Wall can seal off her escape route mid-engage. A landed freeze stops her cold before Vermillion Ascent becomes a threat. Anran can try baiting the slow with Dancing Blaze's brief dodge window before committing to her real engage.
counters.Sombra > Anran: Anran leans on Inferno Rush's charges and Dancing Blaze's dodge window to survive at close range, but Hack denies whichever escape tool she reaches for, leaving her stranded in Machine Pistol range. Stealth also lets Sombra pick the engagement rather than get caught by Anran's own flank. Her counterplay is keeping both Inferno Rush charges topped up so a single hacked ability doesn't strand her.
counters.Sombra > Ashe: Coach Gun is Ashe's only real escape tool, and Hack removes it right as she needs to disengage, leaving her exposed to sustained Machine Pistol fire at close range. Stealth lets Sombra approach undetected and choose the moment to strike before Ashe can land a scoped shot. Ashe's best counterplay is holding Dynamite as a deterrent to keep Sombra from closing safely.
counters.Sombra > Bastion: Configuration: Assault turns Bastion into a stationary damage machine, and Hack denies him the ability to swap configurations or throw A-36 Tactical Grenade at the worst possible moment, freezing him in place. Stealth lets Sombra flank past his forward-facing arc entirely, and up close the Machine Pistol out-trades his fixed positioning. His only real answer is staying mobile in Recon mode rather than committing to Assault, where Hack punishes hardest.
counters.Soldier: 76 > Echo: Echo's Flight and Sticky Bombs demand she stay airborne and exposed, and Soldier: 76's Heavy Pulse Rifle tracks her mid-air with consistent hitscan pressure her low health can't absorb. Tactical Visor further guarantees his shots land the instant she's visible, punishing whatever flight arc she takes. Echo's best chance is using Focusing Beam from cover rather than committing to open-air engagements.
counters.Sojourn > Emre: Emre's Synthetic Burst Rifle and Siphon Blaster both reward holding mid-range engagements, but Sojourn's Disruptor Shot punishes him for standing still, and her charged Railgun secondary fire outpaces his burst rifle before he can trade back. Overclock lets her finish him with repeated rail shots the moment Cyber Frag misses. Emre's counterplay is using Cyber Frag's bounce to pressure her from an angle before she builds rail charge.
counters.Sombra > Freja: Freja needs a stable position to line up Take Aim, and Hack interrupts Updraft or Quick Dash exactly when she's trying to reposition for a shot, leaving her grounded and exposed. Stealth lets Sombra get inside the range where the crossbow loses most of its value. Freja's best answer is keeping a Quick Dash charge in reserve to escape the moment she's hacked.
counters.Mei > Genji: Deflect only reflects projectiles, and Mei's slowing beam and icicles chip Genji down without giving Deflect anything worthwhile to bounce back consistently. Once slowed, he loses the mobility Swift Strike depends on, and a landed freeze ends the fight outright. Genji's counterplay is to Swift Strike through Mei before the slow can stack.
counters.Symmetra > Genji: Deflect can only reflect projectile weapons, and Symmetra's beam-based Photon Projector isn't something he can bounce back once it locks on, leaving his main defensive tool useless while she ramps up damage. Sentry Turrets also punish his double jumps and wall climbs by covering angles Deflect can't clear. Genji's best play is closing distance fast with Swift Strike before the beam ramps to full power.
counters.Hanzo > Widowmaker: Both are long-range duelists, but Hanzo's Storm Arrows can burst down Widowmaker's thin 200 health faster than her Widow's Kiss lands a fully scoped headshot, and Lunge gives him an escape she doesn't have in a straight duel. Sonic Arrow can also reveal her position before she gets the first shot off. Widowmaker's counterplay is using Grappling Hook to break line of sight the instant Hanzo spots her.
counters.Hanzo > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's mere 50 health and 150 shields can't survive a fully charged Storm Bow shot or a Storm Arrows volley, and the damage often outpaces what Orb of Harmony can offset. Sonic Arrow also strips away the cover Zenyatta relies on to hold line of sight for his own orbs. His best defense is keeping the healing orb on himself and breaking line of sight rather than trading shots.
counters.Hanzo > Bastion: Configuration: Assault forces Bastion to stand still to maximize his damage, which makes him an easy target for a fully charged Storm Bow shot, and Sonic Arrow reveals his position before he can set up. Storm Arrows also punish the moments he's locked into a configuration swap. Bastion's counterplay is relocating constantly in Recon mode rather than committing to a fixed Assault position.
counters.Sombra > Hanzo: Hanzo has no way to shake a target once Hack lands, and beyond Lunge he has little to break line of sight quickly, so Sombra's Machine Pistol beats his slow-drawing Storm Bow before he can fully charge a shot. Stealth lets her close the gap he relies on to keep enemies at arrow range. Hanzo's best chance is using Lunge immediately to reset distance and reclaim his range advantage.
counters.Junkrat > Reinhardt: Reinhardt's Rocket Hammer and Barrier Field mean little against a Frag Launcher, whose grenades arc over the barrier to land splash damage regardless of where the shield is held. Steel Trap can also punish a Charge attempt by rooting him mid-dash, and Concussion Mine adds burst on top. Reinhardt's counterplay is closing the gap fast with Charge before the grenades can bounce into range.
counters.Junkrat > Roadhog: Roadhog's 650 health looks daunting, but Frag Launcher splash ignores his lack of armor, and Steel Trap punishes any Chain Hook attempt by rooting him the moment he commits to the pull animation. Concussion Mine adds enough burst to finish what the grenades started before Take a Breather can offset it. Roadhog's best answer is breaking line of sight and healing rather than trading grenade for grenade.
counters.Junkrat > Mei: Ice Wall and Cryo-Freeze are built to control space and buy time, but Junkrat's grenades arc over walls and Concussion Mine can dislodge Mei from behind cover entirely. Steel Trap also punishes her for holding a chokepoint since she has no burst mobility to avoid it. Mei's counterplay is triggering Cryo-Freeze the instant a mine detonates nearby to avoid the follow-up.
counters.Pharah > Junkrat: Frag Launcher and Concussion Mine both rely on ground-level bounce trajectories that struggle to track Pharah once she's airborne with Jump Jet, while her Rocket Launcher's splash punishes him just as hard from a height he can't easily return fire to. Barrage finishes him before RIP-Tire can climb high enough to matter. Junkrat's best chance is using Concussion Mine to launch himself to elevated ground and contest her angle directly.
counters.Mei > Tracer: Blink charges let Tracer dodge most projectiles, but Mei's beam is sustained damage at close range that's much harder to dodge between blinks, and once slowed, Recall can't restore lost momentum. A landed freeze ends the fight outright since Tracer has no way to cleanse it. Her counterplay is Blinking away the instant the slow starts rather than trying to out-trade the beam.
counters.Mei > Reinhardt: Barrier Field blocks projectiles but not Mei's close-range beam once she's inside striking distance, and Ice Wall can split Reinhardt away from his team, denying Charge and Fire Strike the follow-up support they need.
