A frozen Reinhardt is a free elimination for Mei's team. His best counterplay is holding the barrier up and retreating rather than letting Mei close to freezing range.
counters.Pharah > Mei: Mei's Endothermic Blaster and Ice Wall are entirely ground-based, giving her no way to threaten Pharah once she's hovering with Jump Jet, while Pharah's splash damage hits Mei regardless of Cryo-Freeze's brief invulnerability once it ends. Barrage finishes the job from a height she simply cannot reach. Mei's counterplay is holding Ice Wall to block Pharah's landing zone and force a reposition.
counters.Soldier: 76 > Pharah: Pharah's aerial mobility usually keeps her safe from ground-based threats, but Soldier: 76's Heavy Pulse Rifle is consistent hitscan that tracks her mid-flight, and Tactical Visor guarantees his shots connect the instant she's airborne. Helix Rockets add burst to punish any Jump Jet cooldown window. Pharah's best counterplay is breaking line of sight behind cover rather than hovering in open sightlines.
counters.Reaper > Roadhog: Hellfire Shotguns deal such high close-range burst that even 650 health drains fast once Chain Hook fails to land, and The Reaping heals Reaper back up faster than Take a Breather can offset the trade. Wraith Form also lets him ignore a hook attempt entirely by turning briefly untargetable mid-pull. Roadhog's counterplay is landing the hook first and finishing with the Scrap Gun before Reaper can close in.
counters.Reaper > Mauga: Cardiac Overdrive rewards Mauga for brawling at close range, but Reaper's Hellfire Shotguns out-damage his chainguns in a straight trade, and Wraith Form lets Reaper avoid Overrun's charge entirely by briefly becoming invulnerable. The Reaping's lifesteal also keeps Reaper topped up through Mauga's own sustain. Mauga's best counterplay is landing Overrun's stomp before Reaper can commit to Wraith Form.
counters.Reaper > Junker Queen: Adrenaline Rush rewards Junker Queen for staying in the fight, but Reaper's shotguns burst her down faster than her own damage can heal her back, especially since Wraith Form lets him walk through a Jagged Blade pull untouched. Shadow Step also lets him avoid Carnage's melee range until he's ready to commit. Her counterplay is opening with Commanding Shout for the extra health buffer before engaging.
counters.Pharah > Reaper: Hellfire Shotguns are devastating at close range but fall off hard at distance, and Pharah's aerial mobility keeps her permanently out of that range while her rockets chip him down. Wraith Form and Shadow Step both keep Reaper grounded, giving him no way to close on a hovering target. His counterplay is using Shadow Step to flank from an angle she isn't watching rather than approach head-on.
counters.Shion > Widowmaker: Evade lets Shion close the distance on Widowmaker while shrugging off a shot with the brief overhealth it grants, and once inside range her Execution volleys outdamage what Widowmaker's automatic fire returns. Joyride adds a fast approach option a scoped Widowmaker struggles to track. Widowmaker's counterplay is landing the first shot before Shion reaches Execution range, since a fully charged headshot still outpaces her health.
counters.Shion > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's fragile 50 health and 150 shields can't survive Shion's tight-spread Execution volley at close range, and Evade lets her cross into that range while soaking the first punish shot. Joyride gives her another fast way to close if he tries to kite. His best chance is keeping Orb of Discord's target locked from range rather than letting Shion reach volley distance.
counters.Shion > Ana: Ana relies on keeping distance and landing Sleep Dart before threats close in, but Shion's Evade dash covers ground quickly and grants overhealth that can absorb the dart's follow-up window, letting her reach Execution range. Once close, Ana's Biotic Rifle can't out-damage the tightened volley. Ana's counterplay is landing Sleep Dart the instant Shion commits to the dash, denying the follow-up entirely.
counters.Mei > Shion: Shion depends on Evade and Joyride to stay mobile and keep landing Execution volleys, but Mei's slow blunts that mobility and Ice Wall can cut off her approach route entirely. Once slowed, a freeze locks her in place for a clean elimination. Her counterplay is using Evade's overhealth to survive the initial slow and close the gap before the freeze lands.
counters.Sombra > Shion: Shion's kit is built around darting in and out with Evade and Joyride, but Hack removes whichever mobility tool she needs to escape once overextended, stranding her in Machine Pistol range. Stealth also lets Sombra pick when to engage rather than get caught by Shion's flank. Her counterplay is keeping Evade held in reserve as an emergency escape rather than using it aggressively.
counters.Sierra > Pharah: Tracking Shot tags a target for improved follow-up accuracy, and once tagged, the Helix Rifle's accuracy-scaling sustained fire can track Pharah's flight patterns better than most weapons manage. Tremor Charge also punishes her for hovering near any surface within blast range. Pharah's counterplay is breaking tracking with cover between Jump Jet bursts rather than hovering in open sightlines.
counters.Sierra > Echo: Echo depends on Flight and Sticky Bombs to stay airborne and unpredictable, but Tracking Shot marks her the instant she's exposed, letting the Helix Rifle's ramping accuracy punish her mid-air. Anchor Drone also lets Sierra reposition to keep an angle on her. Echo's counterplay is using Focusing Beam from cover rather than committing to open flight paths Sierra can track.
counters.Sierra > Mercy: Mercy has no way to fight back beyond her sidearm, and Tracking Shot lets Sierra follow Guardian Angel repositioning with accurate, sustained Helix Rifle fire that punishes every moment Mercy reveals herself to heal. Tremor Charge can also displace her away from the ally she's tethered to. Mercy's counterplay is keeping line of sight broken and never lingering in a tracked sightline.
counters.Sombra > Sierra: Sierra needs Anchor Drone and Tracking Shot to keep her positioning and pressure consistent, and Hack denies whichever tool she reaches for right as she tries to reposition, leaving her stuck in the open. Stealth lets Sombra bypass her drone's coverage entirely to strike from an unexpected angle. Sierra's counterplay is deploying Anchor Drone early so she always has an escape route ready.
counters.Sojourn > Pharah: Railgun secondary fire is a high-damage hitscan shot that punishes Pharah for holding a predictable hover angle, and once energy is built, Overclock lets Sojourn land repeated rail shots faster than Hover Jets can carry Pharah to full cover. Disruptor Shot also denies the airspace she likes to occupy. Pharah's counterplay is breaking sightlines constantly rather than holding one angle long enough for a charged shot.
counters.Sojourn > Echo: Echo's low health can't survive a charged Railgun shot, and Power Slide keeps Sojourn mobile enough to track Echo's unpredictable Flight paths while building energy off primary fire. Overclock finishes the job with repeated rail shots the instant Echo commits to an open flight line. Echo's counterplay is using Focusing Beam from behind cover rather than exposing herself mid-flight.
counters.Sojourn > Mercy: Mercy's only self-defense is a weak sidearm, and a charged Railgun shot punishes her for staying tethered to an ally in the open. Disruptor Shot also denies the safe repositioning lanes Mercy relies on with Guardian Angel. Mercy's counterplay is staying behind cover and only revealing herself in short bursts to heal.
counters.Sojourn > Widowmaker: In a straight duel, Railgun secondary fire deals comparable burst to Widowmaker's scoped shot but comes with a faster follow-up via Overclock, and Power Slide gives Sojourn an evasive option Widowmaker lacks once spotted. Disruptor Shot can also punish her for holding a static sniping position. Widowmaker's counterplay is landing the first shot and repositioning immediately with Grappling Hook.
counters.Sombra > Sojourn: Sojourn needs sustained primary fire to build energy for her dangerous Railgun secondary shot, and Hack interrupts that rhythm right as she's charging, resetting her threat entirely. Stealth lets Sombra close the gap Power Slide can't create enough separation to escape. Sojourn's counterplay is keeping Disruptor Shot ready to zone Sombra out before she gets close enough to hack.
counters.Soldier: 76 > Mercy: Mercy has almost no way to defend herself directly, and the Heavy Pulse Rifle punishes every moment she exposes herself to heal or resurrect an ally, with Tactical Visor guaranteeing the kill shot the instant she's visible. Helix Rockets also cut off her Guardian Angel escape route. Mercy's counterplay is using Guardian Angel to duck behind cover the moment his ultimate is active.
counters.Sombra > Widowmaker: Widowmaker relies on holding angles from a distance, and Stealth lets Sombra approach unseen before Hack shuts down Grappling Hook right when Widowmaker tries to escape. At close range, the Machine Pistol out-trades her automatic fire and her thin 200 health can't take the sustained damage. Widowmaker's counterplay is repositioning constantly rather than holding a single sniping perch.
counters.Sombra > Zenyatta: Orb of Harmony can't keep up with Sombra's burst once Hack denies Zenyatta's ability to reposition or trigger Transcendence at the critical moment, and his fragile 50 health and 150 shields evaporate under sustained Machine Pistol fire. Stealth also lets Sombra bypass whatever backline cover he's relying on. His counterplay is keeping Transcendence charged as insurance against a surprise dive.
counters.Mei > Sombra: Stealth and Translocator are Sombra's only real safety net, and Mei's beam slows her the instant she reveals herself to attack, denying the escape window Translocator depends on. A landed freeze finishes her before Hack or Virus can be reapplied. Sombra's counterplay is Hacking Mei's Cryo-Freeze before engaging so she can't stall the fight.
counters.Symmetra > Sigma: Kinetic Grasp can absorb a burst of Hyperspheres but carries a lengthy cooldown, leaving Sigma without a shield against Symmetra's sustained, ramping beam for most of the fight. Sentry Turrets add persistent chip damage whenever Kinetic Grasp is down, and Photon Barrier can block his Gravitic Flux setup outright. Sigma's counterplay is holding Experimental Barrier between himself and Symmetra so the beam never connects.
counters.Symmetra > Winston: Barrier Projector blocks direct fire, but Symmetra's Sentry Turrets can be placed to bypass it and chip Winston from an angle, while her ramping beam punishes any Jump Pack dive that brings him into sustained contact range. Photon Barrier can also seal off his engage route entirely. Winston's counterplay is destroying the turrets with Tesla Cannon's cleave before committing to a dive.
counters.Pharah > Symmetra: Photon Projector and Sentry Turrets are both short-range tools, giving Symmetra no way to threaten Pharah once she's hovering above their effective range with Jump Jet. Pharah's splash damage punishes her thin 125 health and 125 shields from a distance her kit was never built to answer. Symmetra's counterplay is using Photon Barrier to block incoming rockets and buy her team time to reposition.
counters.Junkrat > Symmetra: Symmetra's shields are thin and her turrets do little against arcing explosives, so Frag Launcher can chip her down from range where her ramping beam has no chance to connect. Concussion Mine finishes the job before Teleporter or Photon Barrier can be repositioned to help. Her counterplay is keeping Photon Barrier in reserve to block the grenade volley outright.
counters.Pharah > Torbjörn: Deploy Turret and the Rivet Gun are both ground-based and struggle to track a target hovering with Jump Jet, and Pharah's splash damage hits both Torbjörn and his turret from a safe height. Barrage finishes what the rockets started before Overload's speed boost can close the distance. Torbjörn's counterplay is angling his turret toward likely hover spots rather than only covering ground-level sightlines.
counters.Pharah > Vendetta: Vendetta's entire kit — Whirlwind Dash, Projected Edge, Soaring Slice — is built for close-range dueling, and none of it reaches Pharah once she's hovering with Jump Jet outside sword range. Her splash damage punishes any attempt to close the gap, and Barrage finishes her before Sundering Blade can ever connect. Vendetta's counterplay is using Soaring Slice to close distance before Pharah gains full altitude.
counters.Mei > Vendetta: Vendetta depends on closing the gap with Whirlwind Dash and Soaring Slice to land her sword combos, but Mei's slow saps the momentum those dashes rely on, and Ice Wall can block her approach lane entirely. A landed freeze ends the duel before Warding Stance ever comes into play. Vendetta's counterplay is using Warding Stance to absorb the opening hits and close distance regardless.
counters.Pharah > Venture: Burrow and Drill Dash are ground-based tools that can't reach Pharah once she's airborne, and her splash damage punishes Venture the moment they resurface from Burrow. Barrage finishes the job before Tectonic Shock's ground-based shockwaves can threaten her. Venture's counterplay is burrowing toward elevated terrain to contest her angle directly.
counters.Mei > Venture: Drill Dash and Burrow rely on committing to a direction at speed, but Mei's beam slows Venture the moment they resurface, and Ice Wall can trap them mid-approach. A freeze ends the engagement before Clobber's enhanced melee can land. Venture's counterplay is burrowing away rather than continuing the approach once slowed.
counters.Sombra > Ana: Ana relies on keeping distance and landing Sleep Dart before threats close in, but Stealth lets Sombra approach undetected, and Hack denies the dart or Biotic Grenade right as Ana tries to peel. Once in Machine Pistol range, her Biotic Rifle can't out-damage the sustained fire. Ana's counterplay is pre-emptively landing Sleep Dart on suspected flank routes rather than waiting to react.
counters.Sombra > Baptiste: Immortality Field can save Baptiste's team from a burst, but it does nothing to stop Hack from disabling Regenerative Burst at the moment he needs it, leaving him exposed to Sombra's Machine Pistol at close range. Stealth also lets her bypass his Shoulder Turret's automatic fire by staying undetected until she engages. Baptiste's counterplay is keeping Exo Boots ready to jump to safety rather than stand and trade.
counters.Reaper > Brigitte: Barrier Shield and Shield Bash are built for frontal engagements, but Reaper's Wraith Form lets him bypass the barrier from an angle, and his shotguns burst her down before Inspire's passive healing can offset the damage. Shadow Step also lets him flank around Whip Shot's range entirely. Brigitte's counterplay is holding Shield Bash in reserve to stun him the instant he reveals himself from Wraith Form.
counters.Junkrat > Brigitte: Barrier Shield blocks direct fire but not Junkrat's arcing grenades, which land splash damage over the top of it, and Steel Trap punishes a Shield Bash charge by rooting her mid-dash. Concussion Mine adds enough burst to finish her before Repair Pack can offset the damage. Brigitte's counterplay is closing the distance quickly so Junkrat can't safely land his lobbed shots.
counters.Pharah > Brigitte: Whip Shot and Shield Bash are both grounded tools with no way to reach Pharah once she's hovering with Jump Jet, and her splash damage punishes Brigitte from a height Rocket Flail can't threaten. Barrage finishes the job before Rally's overhealth can meaningfully help. Brigitte's counterplay is staying near cover so Pharah can't safely commit to a hover angle above her.
counters.Sombra > Illari: Healing Pylon and the Solar Rifle both require Illari to hold a position, and Stealth lets Sombra close in unseen before Hack shuts down Outburst right as she tries to create separation. Once in range, the Machine Pistol overwhelms her before her healing beam can offset the damage. Illari's counterplay is keeping Outburst ready to knock Sombra back the moment she's spotted.
counters.Hanzo > Jetpack Cat: Jetpack Cat is permanently airborne thanks to the Jetpack passive, which makes them a constant, predictable target for a fully charged Storm Bow shot, and Sonic Arrow reveals their position through cover they might otherwise use to break line of sight. Storm Arrows also punish the moments Frenetic Flight carries them into open sightlines. Their counterplay is using Lifeline to stay close to allies who can screen the angle.
counters.Sombra > Jetpack Cat: Jetpack Cat has no way to become untargetable, so Hack lands easily and shuts down Frenetic Flight or Lifeline right as they try to reposition, leaving them stuck in open air for the Machine Pistol. Stealth lets Sombra close the distance before Purr's healing pulse can offset the damage. Their counterplay is staying near allies who can intercept Sombra before she gets into range.
counters.Sombra > Juno: Glide Boost keeps Juno airborne and hard to pin down, but Hack grounds her double jump and hover right when she needs to escape, and Sombra's Machine Pistol punishes her thin 175 health and 50 shields once she's stuck in the open. Stealth lets Sombra close the gap before Hyper Ring's speed boost helps her teammates arrive. Juno's counterplay is keeping Pulsar Torpedoes ready to punish Sombra's approach.
counters.Reaper > Kiriko: Protection Suzu can cleanse debuffs and grant brief invulnerability, but it can't undo the burst Reaper deals once he's already closed the distance with Shadow Step, and Wraith Form lets him ignore her Kunai on the approach. His shotguns out-damage what Healing Ofuda can offset in real time. Kiriko's counterplay is using Swift Step to reposition to a teammate before Reaper closes fully.
counters.Sombra > Kiriko: Swift Step and Protection Suzu are both interrupted by Hack landing at the wrong moment, stranding Kiriko without an escape or cleanse when Sombra closes in. Stealth also lets Sombra bypass the wall-climbing repositioning Kiriko normally uses to create distance. Her counterplay is tracking Protection Suzu's cooldown so she can cleanse the hack the instant it lands on an ally.
counters.Sombra > Lifeweaver: Life Grip and Petal Platform are both disabled the instant Hack lands, denying Lifeweaver the ability to pull an endangered ally or escape upward when Sombra closes in. Stealth lets her approach without triggering Parting Gift's flower drop prematurely. Lifeweaver's counterplay is using Rejuvenating Dash the moment he sees Sombra decloak, since it isn't caught in the same interrupt window.
counters.Sombra > Mercy: Mercy has essentially no way to fight back once Guardian Angel and Resurrect are shut down by Hack, and Stealth lets Sombra close the distance unseen before finishing her off with the Machine Pistol at close range. Even Valkyrie's enhanced abilities are worthless if Hack lands right as she activates it. Mercy's counterplay is staying near allies who can peel Sombra off before she gets close enough to hack.
counters.Sombra > Mizuki: Katashiro Return and Binding Chain both require an active cooldown to escape or control space, and Hack denies whichever tool Mizuki needs at the moment Sombra closes in, leaving him stuck for the Machine Pistol. Stealth lets her bypass the paper doll teleport entirely by never being where he expects. His counterplay is keeping Healing Kasa's bounce healing active so allies can help finish the fight in his favor.
counters.Pharah > Mizuki: Spirit Glaive and Binding Chain are both ground-level tools with no vertical reach, giving Mizuki no way to threaten Pharah once she's hovering with Jump Jet. Her splash damage hits him regardless of Katashiro Return's repositioning, and Barrage finishes the job from an angle his kit can't answer. His counterplay is using Binding Chain on a diving ally to pull Pharah into a more contestable range.
counters.Pharah > Moira: Biotic Grasp is a short-range beam that does nothing against a target hovering out of reach, and Moira's only escape tool, Fade, doesn't grant altitude, so she can't disengage from Pharah's rocket chip damage. Barrage punishes her for standing near allies she's trying to heal on the ground. Moira's counterplay is using Fade to break line of sight behind solid cover rather than trying to outrun the rockets in the open.
counters.Sombra > Wuyang: Restorative Stream and Rushing Torrent both draw from an active resource that Hack can interrupt at the worst moment, stranding Wuyang without sustain or mobility when Sombra closes in. Stealth lets her bypass Guardian Wave's knockback by never revealing herself until she's already in range. His counterplay is keeping Tidal Blast's overhealth ready as insurance against a surprise dive.
counters.Ana > Doomfist: Doomfist's kit is built around closing distance fast and bursting a target down before they can react. Ana's Sleep Dart has enough range to interrupt that engage before he's in melee range, and landing it turns his all-in into a free kill for her team. He can play around it by baiting the dart out with Seismic Slam first, but that costs him the tempo his kit depends on.
counters.Ana > Domina: Domina's Reconstruction passive only refills her shields when she lands Crystal Charge or Sonic Repulsors, so Ana's Biotic Grenade shutting off healing on anyone caught in it also stops that regeneration cold. Sleep Dart punishes her for poking forward with the Photon Magnum to charge its heavy shot, since she has no quick escape once it lands. Domina can mitigate this by staying behind a Barrier Array panel rather than exposing herself to land Sonic Repulsors.
counters.Ana > Hazard: Hazard wants to close with Violent Leap and brawl at point-blank with Bonespur, but Ana's Sleep Dart has the range to catch him mid-leap before he ever gets there. Once he's grounded, Biotic Grenade's anti-heal strips away any value from Spike Guard's return damage, leaving him with nothing but a shotgun trade he loses. His best counterplay is using Jagged Wall to block her sightline before committing to the leap.
counters.Ana > Junker Queen: Junker Queen's Adrenaline Rush passive heals her off every wound she inflicts, which makes Ana's anti-healing Biotic Grenade a direct answer — it shuts off the lifesteal that keeps her alive in extended Carnage swings. Sleep Dart can also stop a Rampage charge or a Jagged Blade pull before it connects, turning her commitment into a wasted cooldown. She can offset some of this by using Commanding Shout's speed to close the sleep-dart range faster.
counters.Ana > Mauga: Mauga's entire kit leans on sustain — Cardiac Overdrive's damage-to-heal conversion and the overhealth from his Berserker passive — and Ana's Biotic Grenade removes all of it in one throw. Sleep Dart is also strong here since it can catch him during an Overrun charge, stopping the stomp before it lands. His counterplay is baiting the grenade with Cardiac Overdrive's activation timing so his team can punish Ana's cooldown.
counters.Ana > Ramattra: Ramattra relies on Nemesis Form's armour and punching pressure to force fights, but Ana's Sleep Dart can catch him mid-transformation or right as he commits to a Ravenous Vortex push, wasting the cooldown entirely. Her Biotic Grenade also strips any team healing propping him up while he tanks damage in his heavier form. He can play around it by using Void Barrier to block her sightline before engaging.
counters.Ana > Roadhog: Roadhog's survivability hinges on Take a Breather, and Ana's Biotic Grenade denies that self-heal at the exact moment he needs it after whiffing or landing Chain Hook. Sleep Dart is also a clean punish for his hook cooldown window, since he has no mobility tool to escape it. He can try to bait the dart with a hook attempt from an odd angle to force Ana to commit early.
counters.Brigitte > Anran: Anran's Inferno Rush and Dancing Blaze are built to get her into range and keep her alive while she burns a target down, but Brigitte's Shield Bash stuns her out of that dash and her Barrier Shield eats the Zhuque Fan projectiles that build her Ignition pressure. Whip Shot can also knock her away before Fan the Flames finishes securing the burn. Anran can counter by saving Dancing Blaze specifically to dodge the stun rather than for damage.
counters.Ana > Bastion: Bastion is at his most dangerous — and most immobile — while locked into Configuration: Assault or lining up Configuration: Artillery, which gives Ana a clean window to land Sleep Dart and shut the whole thing down instantly. Her Biotic Grenade also removes the healing from his Self-Repair perk, so he can't just tank through the trade. He can mitigate this by only transforming behind cover where Ana lacks line of sight.
counters.Widowmaker > Cassidy: Cassidy's Peacekeeper is a close-to-mid-range duelist's weapon, and Widowmaker's scoped headshots simply outrange it before he can ever get a shot off. Combat Roll gives him mobility to reload, but it doesn't close the gap far enough to matter against a sniper holding an angle. His best answer is using cover to approach and saving Flashbang to punish her if she's caught reloading up close.
counters.Widowmaker > Echo: Echo's Flight and Sticky Bombs demand she get into mid-range to threaten with Focusing Beam, but that puts her squarely in Widowmaker's scoped sightline the entire time she's airborne. A single headshot ends the engagement before Echo's execute damage ever becomes relevant. She can survive by using Flight's verticality to break line of sight rather than approaching in a straight line.
counters.Widowmaker > Emre: Emre's Synthetic Burst Rifle is built for close-to-mid pressure, which means he has to cross a lot of open ground to be effective — ground Widowmaker can punish with a scoped headshot before Cyber Frag or Siphon Blaster ever come into play. His only real answer is using cover and the mobility from Siphon Blaster to close the distance in short, unpredictable bursts.
counters.Tracer > Freja: Freja's Take Aim rewards holding distance and lining up a precise shot, but Tracer's Blink lets her close that gap in an instant and fight at the point-blank range where Pulse Pistols dominate. Recall also lets Tracer reset after a bad engage and try again, wearing down Freja's cooldowns for Quick Dash and Updraft. Freja can play around it by using Updraft proactively to deny the flank angle before Tracer arrives.
counters.Widowmaker > Junkrat: Junkrat's Frag Launcher is a bouncing, close-to-mid area weapon with no long-range answer, which leaves him completely exposed to Widowmaker picking him off from a sightline he can't threaten back. Steel Trap and Concussion Mine are no use against a target that never gets close enough to trigger them. His best option is using terrain to break line of sight while repositioning toward the objective.
counters.Widowmaker > Mei: Mei's Endothermic Blaster only becomes dangerous once she's within beam range, and Widowmaker's headshot potential lets her end the fight before Mei ever gets that close. Cryo-Freeze can save her from a follow-up shot, but not from the first pick landing clean. Mei can offset this by using Ice Wall to block sightlines while advancing rather than walking directly into the open.
counters.Widowmaker > Pharah: Pharah's Rocket Launcher rewards aerial unpredictability, but a hitscan headshot from Widowmaker doesn't care how she's moving — it lands the instant the crosshair is on target, unlike Pharah's own travel-time projectiles. Jump Jet and Concussive Blast help her juke, but neither removes her from a sniper's sightline entirely. She can mitigate the matchup by hugging cover and only exposing herself in short bursts.
counters.Ana > Reaper: Reaper needs to be in Wraith Form or already closing the gap for his shotguns to matter, and Ana's Sleep Dart has the range to shut that engage down before he arrives. Biotic Grenade is just as important here, since it cancels The Reaping's lifesteal and removes the sustain that lets him win close-range trades. He can try to use Shadow Step to approach from an angle Ana isn't watching.
counters.Widowmaker > Reaper: Reaper has no ranged tool beyond Shadow Step to close distance, which leaves him fully exposed to Widowmaker's scoped headshots while he's still crossing the map. By the time Wraith Form gets him into shotgun range, he's usually already been picked off. His only real counterplay is using Shadow Step aggressively to skip the open ground entirely.
counters.Brigitte > Shion: Shion's Kira Pistols and Execution volleys want her in close where Brigitte's Barrier Shield can simply block the burst, and Shield Bash's stun interrupts her Evade dash before she can reposition. Repair Pack and the passive Inspire healing also let Brigitte's team out-sustain the pressure Shion is trying to apply. Shion can play around it by using Joyride to disengage the moment the barrier goes up.
counters.Widowmaker > Sierra: Sierra's Helix Rifle needs sustained fire to ramp up its accuracy, which means she has to commit to an exposed firefight that Widowmaker can end first with a single scoped headshot. Anchor Drone gives her some repositioning, but not enough to close a sniper's range advantage safely. Her best bet is using Tremor Charge to force Widowmaker off an angle before advancing.
counters.Widowmaker > Soldier: 76: 76's Heavy Pulse Rifle is strong at mid-range, but that's exactly the zone where Widowmaker's headshots already outclass him, and Sprint doesn't help him close a sniper's distance advantage. Biotic Field can keep him topped up, but not if he's getting picked before he's even in his effective range. He can mitigate the matchup by using Helix Rockets and cover to poke rather than approach directly.
counters.Brigitte > Sombra: Sombra depends on Hack and Translocator flanks to isolate a target, but Brigitte's Shield Bash can stun her the instant she reveals herself, and Barrier Shield blocks the Virus projectile she needs to secure the kill. Repair Pack sustain also undoes much of the chip damage Sombra builds up before her Hack lands. Sombra can play around it by using Stealth to bait out the barrier before committing to the real engage.
counters.Torbjörn > Tracer: Torbjörn's Deploy Turret tracks automatically and punishes exactly the kind of short, repeated engagements Tracer relies on, since her low 175 health can't absorb sustained turret fire on top of a trade. Overload also gives him the reload and fire-rate spike to finish her off if she commits to a close-range duel. Tracer can mitigate this by using Blink to break the turret's line of sight before it locks onto her.
counters.Torbjörn > Genji: Torbjörn's turret provides constant tracking pressure that Genji's Deflect can't answer, since it isn't a reflectable projectile burst the way Dragonblade swings are meant to counter. Even as Genji dashes in with Swift Strike, the turret keeps chipping him down, and Overload lets Torbjörn finish the trade with a faster-firing Rivet Gun. Genji can play around it by approaching from an angle outside the turret's cone before committing to Deflect.
counters.Torbjörn > Sombra: Sombra's Stealth and Translocator are built to set up a clean pick, but a placed turret keeps firing on sightlines she has to cross, making a quiet flank much harder to pull off. If she does get caught, Torbjörn's Overload gives him enough burst to finish her off before Virus does its work. She can mitigate this by hacking or destroying the turret from stealth before attempting her main engage.
counters.Torbjörn > Pharah: Torbjörn's Rivet Gun reaches out further than most primary weapons in the Damage roster, and his turret can track Pharah through her hover patterns just as easily as a grounded target. That consistent chip damage punishes the predictable arcs she needs to fly to land rockets accurately. Pharah can offset this by breaking line of sight with terrain between Jump Jet uses.
counters.Widowmaker > Torbjörn: Torbjörn has no reliable long-range weapon or mobility tool, so once Widowmaker spots him she can pick him from well outside Rivet Gun's effective range, and his turret can't reposition to help. Overload gives him a brief damage boost but nothing that closes a sniper's distance advantage. His best answer is playing off Deploy Turret's cover and avoiding open sightlines entirely.
counters.Tracer > Widowmaker: Widowmaker's entire kit depends on maintaining range, and Tracer's Blink erases that distance in an instant, putting her inside Widow's 200 health pool before Grappling Hook can create separation. Once there, Pulse Pistols delete her far faster than Widow's automatic fire can respond. Widowmaker's main answer is Venom Mine placed on likely flank routes to reveal Tracer before she closes in.
counters.Tracer > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's 50 health and 150 shields sit on a hero with no mobility ability, which makes him one of the easiest targets for Tracer's Blink to reach and burst down with Pulse Pistols before Transcendence can be activated. Recall lets her retry safely if the first attempt goes wrong. His only real defense is keeping Orb of Discord and Destruction ready to punish her the instant she reveals herself.
counters.Tracer > Ana: Ana's Sleep Dart needs a clean read and travel time to land, which gives Tracer's Blink enough of a window to close the gap before it ever gets thrown. Once she's on top of Ana, the 250 health pool folds quickly to Pulse Pistols with no armour to soften it. Ana's best counterplay is pre-firing Sleep Dart toward common flank paths rather than reacting after Tracer is already close.
counters.Tracer > Ashe: Ashe's Viper rifle is strongest at range, but Tracer's Blink lets her dive inside that effective distance where the rifle's damage falls off and her own burst takes over. Coach Gun can knock Tracer away, but its cooldown means it isn't always up when the dive happens. Ashe can mitigate this by holding Dynamite as a punish for predictable Blink approach lanes.
counters.Brigitte > Tracer: Tracer wants to isolate a backline target and burst them down with Pulse Pistols, but Brigitte's Whip Shot and Shield Bash both interrupt that engage at range and up close, while Inspire's passive healing keeps her allies topped up so there's no clean pick to be had. Repair Pack can also undo most of the chip damage Tracer manages to land. Tracer's best play is baiting Shield Bash out before committing to the real dive.
counters.Vendetta > Widowmaker: Vendetta's Soaring Slice lets her close distance onto an exposed sniping angle almost instantly, and once she's there, Widowmaker has no answer beyond Grappling Hook to escape a duelist built for point-blank sword pressure. Sundering Blade can finish the job even if Widow tries to reposition mid-fight. Widowmaker's best counterplay is relocating constantly and using Venom Mine to reveal Vendetta's approach early.
counters.Vendetta > Zenyatta: Zenyatta has no mobility to create space, so Vendetta's Whirlwind Dash and Soaring Slice reach him well before Orb of Discord or Destruction can do meaningful damage back. Sundering Blade also cuts straight through his shields once she's in range. His only real defense is keeping distance behind allies who can intercept her approach.
counters.Vendetta > Ana: Ana's Sleep Dart is a precise, telegraphed tool, and Vendetta's mobility kit — Soaring Slice and Whirlwind Dash together — closes the distance faster than Ana can react to land it. Once in melee range, her sword pressure overwhelms Ana's 250 health with no shield to soften it. Ana can play around this by pre-emptively landing Sleep Dart the moment Vendetta commits to an engage animation.
counters.Brigitte > Vendetta: Vendetta's damage depends on chaining melee strikes together, but Brigitte's Shield Bash stuns her out of that rhythm the instant she closes in, and Barrier Shield can absorb a Projected Edge before it lands. Repair Pack sustain also outpaces the burst Vendetta needs to win the duel quickly. Vendetta can mitigate this by using Warding Stance to punish Brigitte's melee swings rather than trading directly.
counters.Venture > Widowmaker: Venture's Burrow makes them completely untargetable while closing the gap on a sniping angle, so Widowmaker's usual range advantage simply doesn't apply until it's too late. Clobber and Drill Dash finish the job the moment Venture surfaces next to her. Widowmaker's best defense is repositioning often and watching for burrow tremors telegraphing the approach.
counters.Venture > Zenyatta: Venture can Burrow straight past Zenyatta's Orb of Destruction and Discord without taking any damage, surfacing at melee range where his lack of mobility leaves him with no way to create separation. Clobber's enhanced melee finishes him quickly once Venture emerges. His only real counterplay is staying near allies who can punish the burrow the moment it surfaces.
counters.Venture > Ana: Ana's Sleep Dart is Venture's only real threat at range, but Burrow makes them untargetable while closing in, completely bypassing the dart's window entirely. Once they surface next to her, Clobber and the Smart Excavator finish the trade before she can peel. Ana can mitigate this by tracking burrow tremors and pre-firing Sleep Dart toward where they're likely to emerge.
counters.Widowmaker > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's core sits at just 50 health behind 150 shields, and a single scoped headshot from Widowmaker deletes him outright before Orb of Harmony or Discord ever factor into the fight. He has no mobility tool to break her sightline either. His best chance is staying tucked behind allies and cover rather than holding an open angle.
counters.Widowmaker > Ana: Both are backline snipers, but in a direct duel Widowmaker's scoped headshot kills Ana's 250 health outright in one shot, while Ana's Biotic Rifle needs multiple body shots — or a well-telegraphed Sleep Dart — to answer back in time. Ana's best counterplay is using Sleep Dart proactively on sightlines rather than reacting after Widow already has an angle on her.
counters.Ana > Wrecking Ball: Wrecking Ball's Piledriver and Grappling Claw combo needs him to commit fully to an aerial swing, and Ana's Sleep Dart can catch him the moment he lands or right as Adaptive Shield expires, denying the follow-up. Biotic Grenade also removes any healing propping him up during an extended dive. He can play around it by using Roll to break line of sight before committing to the engage.
counters.Baptiste > Reaper: Reaper wants to close the distance and secure a kill with Death Blossom or a point-blank shotgun burst, but Baptiste's Immortality Field denies that pick outright, keeping the target alive to punish him once Wraith Form runs out. Regenerative Burst also lets Baptiste's team out-heal the burst damage Reaper needs to win the exchange. His counterplay is destroying the Immortality Field before committing to the all-in.
counters.Baptiste > Roadhog: Roadhog's hook-into-shotgun combo is built to secure a single guaranteed kill, but Baptiste's Immortality Field neutralizes exactly that threat, letting the hooked ally survive the follow-up shot. Regenerative Burst then tops everyone back up before Roadhog can capitalize further. Roadhog can mitigate this by hooking the Immortality Field's target directly to remove the safety net first.
counters.Baptiste > Mauga: Mauga's Cage Fight and Cardiac Overdrive are designed around an all-in commitment, but Baptiste's Immortality Field denies him the pick he needs to make that trade worthwhile, keeping his team alive through the burst. Regenerative Burst then out-sustains the chaingun pressure once the cage drops. Mauga's best counterplay is focusing the Immortality Field device itself before going for kills.
counters.Tracer > Baptiste: Baptiste has little in the way of personal mobility beyond his Exo Boots jump, which makes him an easy target for Tracer's Blink to reach before he can reposition or drop Immortality Field in time. Her burst can often finish him before Regenerative Burst ticks enough healing to matter. Baptiste's best defense is pre-placing Immortality Field near himself whenever a Tracer flank is likely.
counters.Brigitte > Genji: Genji's Swift Strike dive is meant to end in a kill and a cooldown reset, but Brigitte's Shield Bash can stun him mid-dash since it isn't a projectile his Deflect can reflect, cutting the combo short. Repair Pack and Inspire's passive healing then undo whatever chip damage he managed to land. Genji's counterplay is baiting the stun with a feint before committing to the real Dragonblade window.
counters.Illari > Reaper: Reaper needs to close to shotgun range to be dangerous, but Illari's Solar Rifle can pick him off at distance well before he gets there, and Outburst's knockback creates space the instant he tries to force an engage. Healing Pylon also keeps her topped up so a single burst of damage from Reaper doesn't finish the job. His best counterplay is using Shadow Step to bypass her sightline entirely.
counters.Illari > Roadhog: A single Roadhog hook is meant to secure a pick, but Illari's Healing Pylon can quickly restore whatever health the hooked ally lost, and Outburst's knockback disrupts his positioning before he can follow up with Chain Hook again. Her Solar Rifle also punishes him at range while Take a Breather is on cooldown. Roadhog can mitigate this by targeting the Pylon directly to remove the sustain first.
counters.Illari > Pharah: Illari's Solar Rifle is a precision hitscan weapon that tracks Pharah's aerial movement far more reliably than Pharah's own splash damage tracks a moving target on the ground. That consistent poke chips away her health before Barrage becomes a real threat. Pharah can offset this by breaking line of sight between Jump Jet arcs rather than hovering predictably in the open.
counters.Tracer > Illari: Illari's peel tools are limited — Healing Pylon is stationary and Outburst has a real cooldown — which leaves her exposed once Tracer's Blink gets her into range. Tracer's burst usually ends the fight before Captive Sun or the Pylon can turn the tide. Illari's best counterplay is placing her Pylon defensively and keeping Outburst ready rather than using it offensively.
counters.Jetpack Cat > Pharah: Jetpack Cat's permanent flight lets them match Pharah's hover patterns instead of being grounded like most supports, using Biotic Pawjectiles to poke her from the same altitude while Frenetic Flight dodges incoming rockets. Purr's area damage also chips her down passively during extended air duels. Pharah can mitigate this by using Concussive Blast to knock Jetpack Cat out of an advantageous angle.
counters.Jetpack Cat > Echo: Because Jetpack Cat is airborne by default, they can contest Echo's Flight windows directly rather than losing the vertical duel by default, peppering her with pellet spread before she gets into Focusing Beam execute range. Lifeline also lets them pull an ally clear if Echo commits to a dive. Echo's best counterplay is using Sticky Bombs' burst to punish Jetpack Cat before they can react in the air.
counters.Jetpack Cat > Reinhardt: Permanent flight keeps Jetpack Cat well above Reinhardt's Barrier Field and out of Charge or Fire Strike range, letting them poke him down and support allies from angles his hammer can never reach. Purr's passive damage adds up over a long shield-holding standoff. Reinhardt's only real counterplay is using Fire Strike's piercing projectile to punish predictable hover positions.
counters.Widowmaker > Jetpack Cat: Jetpack Cat's slow spread of Biotic Pawjectiles has no answer to a scoped headshot landing from far outside its effective range, and their permanent flight actually makes them an easier target to track in open sky. Widowmaker can pick them off before Purr or Lifeline change the fight. Jetpack Cat's best defense is using terrain to break the sightline rather than staying exposed in the open air.
counters.Juno > Pharah: Juno's Glide Boost lets her double jump and hover right alongside Pharah instead of being stuck fighting from the ground, landing Mediblaster hits while dodging her rockets with the same aerial mobility. Pulsar Torpedoes' homing tracking also chases Pharah through evasive movement that would normally lose a ground-based support. Pharah can mitigate this by breaking line of sight between hover bursts.
counters.Juno > Echo: Juno's aerial mobility lets her chase Echo through Flight rather than being left behind, matching her altitude while Pulsar Torpedoes chip away at her from range. Juno's healing also lets her outlast the burst Echo needs from Focusing Beam to close out a fight. Echo's best counterplay is using Sticky Bombs to punish Juno before she can reposition.
counters.Juno > Reinhardt: Juno's double jump and hover keep her above and around Reinhardt's Barrier Field and Charge lane entirely, letting her poke him down and reposition allies with Hyper Ring from angles his hammer can't threaten. Pulsar Torpedoes' homing also punishes him even when he's tucked behind his own shield. Reinhardt's counterplay is using Fire Strike to pierce through and force her to relocate.
counters.Tracer > Juno: Juno has no hard peel in close range beyond Pulsar Torpedoes' lock-on, which gives Tracer's Blink an easy opening to burst her down before Orbital Ray or the torpedoes can turn the fight around. Recall lets Tracer reset and try again if the first dive is contested. Juno's best defense is using Hyper Ring proactively to keep herself and allies mobile before the flank lands.
counters.Tracer > Lifeweaver: Lifeweaver's healing tools are strong for protecting allies but leave little for himself — Rejuvenating Dash is his only real self-peel — so Tracer's Blink and burst damage can close and finish him before Life Grip or Petal Platform change the outcome. Recall lets her confirm the kill safely if the first attempt is contested. Lifeweaver's counterplay is using Petal Platform preemptively to create an escape route.
counters.Widowmaker > Lúcio: Lúcio's Wall Ride is excellent for closing distance and dodging fire at range, but it does nothing to stop a scoped headshot landing before he's even built up speed, since Widowmaker can pick him the moment he's spotted off a wall. Soundwave's knockback has no reach against a sniper holding a distant angle. Lúcio's best counterplay is keeping cover between himself and open sightlines while rotating.
counters.Tracer > Mercy: Mercy's only real escape is Guardian Angel toward a nearby ally, which gives Tracer's Blink an easy way to cut off her pathing and burst her down before she reaches safety. Recall lets Tracer confirm the kill even if Mercy manages an early dodge. Mercy's best counterplay is staying close to allies who can peel and never letting her Guardian Angel route get isolated.
counters.Widowmaker > Mizuki: Mizuki's Spirit Glaive relies on bounces and proximity to build up its damage over time, which means she has to be relatively close to be effective — well within a range Widowmaker can pick her from first with a scoped headshot. Healing Kasa's sustain doesn't matter if she's eliminated before the bounce heal can land. Mizuki's counterplay is using Katashiro Return to break the engagement the moment she's spotted.
counters.Widowmaker > Moira: Moira's Biotic Grasp only functions at short-to-medium range, so Widowmaker can snipe her from a distance Moira simply has no tool to close safely, since Fade's invulnerability only helps once she's already under fire, not before. A clean headshot ends the exchange before Coalescence is ever an option. Moira's best counterplay is using Fade proactively to close distance unseen rather than approaching in the open.
counters.Tracer > Wuyang: Wuyang has no personal escape beyond the speed boost from Rushing Torrent, which gives Tracer's Blink plenty of opportunity to catch him before Guardian Wave's healing or Tidal Blast's overhealth can offset her burst damage. Recall lets her retry cleanly if the first dive doesn't land. Wuyang's counterplay is using Rushing Torrent proactively to keep distance rather than reacting after Tracer is already close.
counters.Kiriko > Junker Queen: Junker Queen's whole kit — Jagged Blade, Carnage, and her Rampage ultimate — inflicts wounds that block healing, which is exactly the debuff Protection Suzu cleanses on contact while granting a window of invulnerability. That single ability turns her signature anti-heal pressure into a non-event for Kiriko's team, and her Wall Climb plus Swift Step let her stay out of Carnage's melee range while doing it. Junker Queen can try to bait Suzu out early with Jagged Blade before committing to Rampage, but that telegraphs her engage.
counters.Zenyatta > Mauga: Mauga's Cardiac Overdrive sustain relies on dealing damage to heal off it, so when Zenyatta slaps Orb of Discord onto him, every hit he takes gets amplified 30% and his team's return fire outpaces what Cardiac Overdrive can offset. He has no barrier or reflect to strip the orb, and since Zenyatta can maintain Discord from range without dueling him, Mauga is stuck tanking amplified damage the whole engagement. His only real answer is closing the gap with Overrun and forcing Zenyatta to break line of sight.
counters.Zenyatta > Orisa: Orisa's defensive tech — Fortify's damage reduction and Javelin Spin's projectile clear — is built to blunt burst and crowd control, but Orb of Discord is neither; it's a passive damage amplifier that bypasses both, so her armour absorbs less than it should relative to incoming damage. Zenyatta can hold Discord on her from well outside javelin range, meaning she either eats the extra 30% all fight or burns Fortify constantly just to survive normal poke.
counters.Zenyatta > Ramattra: Ramattra leans on Nemesis Form's armour and Void Barrier to trade safely, but neither defends against Orb of Discord's flat 30% damage amplification, so Zenyatta effectively deletes a chunk of that survivability the moment the orb sticks. He has no reliable way to remove it at range, meaning every Ravenous Vortex push or punch combo happens while marked. His best counterplay is timing Nemesis Form's higher effective health to soak the amplified damage rather than avoiding it.
counters.Zenyatta > Roadhog: Roadhog's 650 health pool is meant to absorb pressure over time, but Orb of Discord's 30% amplifier turns every stray hit into meaningfully more damage, and since Zenyatta pokes from range he's rarely in Chain Hook distance to punish. Take a Breather reduces incoming damage but doesn't remove the debuff, so Roadhog bleeds out faster than his self-heal can keep up, especially once Zenyatta's own orb chip is added on top.
counters.Moira > Genji: Genji's entire defensive kit is built around reflecting projectiles with Deflect, but Moira's Biotic Grasp is a locked-on beam rather than a projectile, so it simply can't be sent back at her — he has no answer to her damage while she's tracking him. Fade also lets Moira vanish the instant Genji commits Swift Strike or Dragonblade at her, denying the payoff either dash needs to matter.
counters.Kiriko > Sombra: Sombra's assassination plan depends on Hack landing cleanly to lock down cooldowns before she strikes, and Protection Suzu cleanses that disruption the instant it happens while making nearby allies briefly untouchable. Kunai also punishes Sombra the moment she decloaks to attack, since a clean headshot deals 120 damage against her 250 health, and Swift Step lets Kiriko simply teleport away from a flank that's already been read.
counters.Moira > Tracer: Tracer survives burst by Recalling back to a healthier, better-positioned version of herself, but Moira's Biotic Grasp beam tracks her at close range without requiring precision aim, so the damage keeps landing through every Blink. Fade also gives Moira a clean escape or repositioning tool the instant Tracer tries to combo Pulse Pistols into a kill, denying the exact burst window Tracer's kit is built around.
counters.Kiriko > Zenyatta: Zenyatta's 50 health and 150 shields make him the single squishiest hero in the game, and Kunai's headshot multiplier turns a clean throw into 120 damage — enough that two connect before his shields even start to matter. He has no dash or blink to escape, so Swift Step lets Kiriko close the gap instantly and finish what her thrown kunai started before he can put anything between them.
counters.Kiriko > Ana: Ana's kit revolves around long, deliberate cooldowns — Sleep Dart and Biotic Grenade both punish being caught out of position — but Kiriko's Swift Step erases the neutral-game distance those cooldowns rely on, letting her close in almost instantly. Once she's there, Kunai's burst threatens Ana's 250 health directly, and Ana has no personal escape tool beyond her rifle's range to fall back on.
counters.Kiriko > Illari: Illari holds her ground behind Healing Pylon and her Solar Rifle's range, but she only has Outburst — a single seven-second-cooldown knockback — to create distance once that position is pressured. Swift Step and Kunai let Kiriko dive past that positioning entirely and threaten Illari's 225 health directly, and Protection Suzu can cleanse or shield an ally caught by Captive Sun before it detonates.
counters.Lifeweaver > Roadhog: Roadhog's Chain Hook is built to isolate a backline target and finish them with a point-blank Scrap Gun blast, but Life Grip yanks the hooked ally instantly back to Lifeweaver, cleansing them mid-flight and undoing the setup before Roadhog can capitalize. Petal Platform also gives Lifeweaver a vertical escape route that puts him out of hook range entirely, denying Roadhog the angle he needs.
counters.Lifeweaver > Reinhardt: Reinhardt wants to pin an exposed support with Charge or corner them for a hammer swing, but Life Grip can rip the targeted ally out of that path the instant he commits, wasting the long cooldown for nothing. Petal Platform additionally lets Lifeweaver's team hop to high ground his hammer and barrier can't easily follow, sidestepping the slow, grounded pressure his kit depends on.
counters.Lifeweaver > Doomfist: Doomfist wants to isolate a single squishy target with a Seismic Slam into Rocket Punch combo, but Life Grip can snatch that target away mid-combo, cleansing them of the follow-up before he lands the kill his whole engage was built around. Petal Platform gives Lifeweaver an extra escape route of his own, so even if Doomfist reaches him directly, he isn't cornered.
counters.Lúcio > Reinhardt: Reinhardt's Charge needs a clean, uninterrupted line to pin someone, but Lúcio's Wall Ride keeps him constantly moving and hard to line up, and a well-timed Soundwave can shove Reinhardt off his charge path before it connects. His team's Speed Song also lets them simply outrun the barrier wall Reinhardt is trying to hold them behind.
counters.Lúcio > Doomfist: Doomfist's engage depends on connecting Rocket Punch or Seismic Slam cleanly onto a target, and Lúcio's constant wall-riding mobility makes him an awkward one to land either on. If Doomfist commits anyway, Soundwave can knock him off course mid-punch or push him away entirely, and Lúcio's team is fast enough on Speed Song to already be gone by the time he arrives.
counters.Lúcio > Ramattra: Ramattra's slow, methodical Nemesis Form push wants him in melee range to land his punches, but Lúcio's Speed Song lets his team stay just out of reach while poking him down, and Soundwave can physically shove Ramattra away from the fight the instant he closes the distance. That denies him the sustained brawl his transformation is built for.
counters.Mercy > Pharah: Pharah's aerial advantage usually keeps her safe from grounded support, but Mercy's Guardian Angel lets her fly to match Pharah's altitude and either damage-boost a teammate shooting her down or keep pace to deny a clean escape. Because Mercy is also constantly repositioning mid-air, Pharah's splash-damage Rocket Launcher struggles to land a clean hit on her the way it would on a stationary healer.
counters.Mercy > Echo: Echo wants a wounded, isolated target to execute with Focusing Beam or a Duplicate copy she can spend freely, but Mercy's Regeneration passive and easy access to Flash Heal keep allies out of that low-health window in the first place. Guardian Angel also lets Mercy yank herself or a teammate out of Echo's line the instant Sticky Bombs are thrown, denying the setup entirely.
counters.Mercy > Widowmaker: Widowmaker needs a held sightline and a stationary head to land her one-shot, but Mercy's Guardian Angel keeps her in constant, erratic aerial motion that's difficult to track through a scope. Pocketing a flanker with Damage Boost also lets Mercy's team punish Widowmaker's exposed 200 health before she can reposition with Grappling Hook.
counters.Mizuki > Doomfist: Doomfist's combos depend entirely on stringing Rocket Punch and Seismic Slam together to close and finish a target, but Binding Chain locks onto him and strips away his ability to use those movement tools or even walk out of range, turning his all-in into a stationary target. Katashiro Return also lets Mizuki reposition away from the initial hit before the chain even lands.
counters.Mizuki > Wrecking Ball: Wrecking Ball's entire threat comes from building momentum with Roll and Grappling Claw to slam into a backline, but Binding Chain shuts down his movement abilities the moment it connects, leaving him stranded mid-engage with no way to escape or reset. Healing Kasa's multi-target bounce then keeps Mizuki's team topped up through whatever damage he manages before the chain runs out.
counters.Mizuki > Tracer: Tracer's evasiveness is built entirely around Blink, and Binding Chain removes her ability to use it or even walk away once tethered, turning her signature mobility into a liability. Her 175 health also can't absorb much of the Spirit Glaive's bounce damage plus its lingering damage over time, so a single connected chain often ends the fight outright.
counters.Moira > Reaper: Reaper's Wraith Form makes him briefly untouchable, but he can't fire his Hellfire Shotguns while using it, so Moira simply Fades away and reappears once the invulnerability expires and he's forced back into range. Her Biotic Grasp beam also tracks him without precision aim at exactly the close range his shotguns want to fight at, letting her out-damage his lifesteal from The Reaping.
counters.Wuyang > Reinhardt: Reinhardt commits to long, straight Charge lines to reach backline targets, but Guardian Wave's knockback can shove him off that path entirely before he connects, and Rushing Torrent gives Wuyang the speed to dodge Fire Strike or simply outrun the charge. His Restorative Stream also keeps allies sustained through Reinhardt's hammer poke without Wuyang needing to expose himself in melee range.
counters.Wuyang > Doomfist: Doomfist wants an isolated healer to punish with Seismic Slam into Rocket Punch, but Rushing Torrent gives Wuyang the burst mobility to dash clear before the combo lands, and Guardian Wave's knockback can interrupt his approach outright. Even if he does connect, Wuyang's fast-triggering Survivor passive helps him recover health between engagements.
counters.Wuyang > Pharah: Pharah usually punishes grounded supports who can't answer her aerial poke, but Wuyang's Restorative Stream sustains allies passively at range without him needing to expose his position, and Guardian Wave's 50% healing amplification lets his team out-heal her Rocket Launcher chip far longer than she can sustain the trade. Rushing Torrent also gives him the mobility to break her sightline entirely.
counters.Zenyatta > Reaper: Reaper wants to close into point-blank Hellfire Shotgun range and heal off The Reaping, but Orb of Discord marks him for 30% more damage the moment he commits, meaning the burst he takes on the approach outpaces what his lifesteal can offset. Zenyatta's own Orb of Destruction chip damage also punishes him well before he's even in shotgun range, undercutting the entire premise of his engage.

synergies.D.Va + Winston: Both are dive tanks whose kits reward the same aggressive style, and D.Va's Defense Matrix can screen Winston's Jump Pack approach from ranged punish while he closes in alongside her. Winston's Barrier Projector also gives D.Va a safe pocket to fall back into between engagements. Together they can isolate a backline target from two directions at once, splitting the enemy's attention faster than either could alone.
synergies.D.Va + Tracer: D.Va's Defense Matrix can absorb the burst damage that would otherwise punish Tracer for overextending on a Blink engage, buying her the extra second she needs to land Pulse Bomb. Both heroes share the same dive-and-disrupt game plan, so they naturally end up pressuring the same backline targets together. If the engage goes wrong, D.Va's higher health lets her cover Tracer's Recall window.
synergies.D.Va + Genji: D.Va's Defense Matrix can eat the burst damage that would otherwise punish Genji for committing to a Swift Strike dive, and her mobility means she can follow him into the backline rather than being left behind. Genji's Deflect also protects D.Va's Self-Destruct mech from being shot down before it detonates. The pair overwhelm supports and squishies simultaneously from multiple angles.
synergies.D.Va + Kiriko: Kiriko's Swift Step lets her instantly close the distance to a diving D.Va, keeping her topped up with Healing Ofuda deep in enemy territory. Protection Suzu also cleanses any crowd control that would otherwise interrupt D.Va's engage or Self-Destruct setup. In return, D.Va's presence gives Kiriko a much safer target to teleport toward than a squishy ally.
synergies.Doomfist + Tracer: Doomfist and Tracer both thrive on the same flanking, backline-disruption playstyle, so committing together overwhelms a support duo faster than either could alone. While Doomfist draws attention and cooldowns with his Rocket Punch engage, Tracer can capitalize on the chaos with Pulse Bomb. Their shared mobility also means they can retreat and re-engage in near-perfect sync.
synergies.Doomfist + Sombra: Sombra's Hack disables the exact defensive abilities that would otherwise save a target from Doomfist's all-in combo, like an escape cooldown or a shield, right before he commits Rocket Punch. Her Translocator can also reposition to set up simultaneous engages by extension of denying enemy vision. Together they turn Doomfist's biggest weakness, a telegraphed engage, into a near-guaranteed pick.
synergies.Doomfist + Kiriko: Kiriko's Swift Step lets her follow Doomfist directly into the backline the instant he commits Rocket Punch, keeping him topped up with Healing Ofuda far from her own supports. Protection Suzu also cleanses any crowd control used to punish his dive, like a Sleep Dart or Hack, letting him finish the combo he started. Doomfist in turn clears space for Kiriko to safely reposition.
synergies.Doomfist + Lúcio: Lúcio's Speed Song extends the range Doomfist can cover before committing Rocket Punch, letting him reach targets that would otherwise be safely out of reach. Amp It Up's healing burst or Sound Barrier's overhealth can also cover him during Power Block's vulnerable recovery window. The pairing turns Doomfist into an even faster, harder-to-punish threat.
synergies.Domina + Widowmaker: Barrier Array gives Widowmaker a segmented wall to snipe from behind, letting her hold an angle safely even as individual panels get broken down. Domina's Sonic Repulsors can also punish anyone who dives past the barrier to reach her. The pairing lets Widowmaker play far more aggressively than she normally could without a dedicated barrier tank.
synergies.Domina + Sojourn: Barrier Array gives Sojourn cover to charge up her Railgun's energy safely, protecting the exact vulnerable window her kit revolves around. Domina's Crystal Charge can also poke through gaps to soften targets before Sojourn's Overclock finishes them. Both heroes scale their damage output around sustained, protected positioning, which makes them a natural pairing.
synergies.Domina + Zenyatta: Barrier Array shields Zenyatta's fragile 50 health while he holds Orb of Discord on a priority target, letting Domina's Photon Magnum and his own Orb of Destruction punish the amplified target together. Domina's Sonic Repulsors also knock back anyone diving toward Zenyatta before they reach him. The pair can poke safely from behind cover that neither could maintain alone.
synergies.Hazard + Lúcio: Lúcio's Speed Song lets Hazard chain Violent Leap engages faster than his cooldown alone would allow, closing distance almost continuously. Wall Ride synergizes naturally with Hazard's own vertical mobility, letting the pair reposition around a fight together. Lúcio's healing aura also offsets the damage Hazard takes brawling in Spike Guard range.
synergies.Hazard + Kiriko: Kiriko's Swift Step lets her follow Hazard the instant he commits Violent Leap into the enemy backline, keeping him healed deep in hostile territory. Protection Suzu also cleanses crowd control used to punish his leap, letting him hold the space Jagged Wall carved out. Hazard's aggression gives Kiriko a constant stream of safe teleport targets.
synergies.Hazard + Juno: Juno's Hyper Ring gets Hazard into Violent Leap range faster, letting him close distances that would otherwise require using the ability's cooldown early. Pulsar Torpedoes can then sustain him through the brawl his Bonespur shotgun and Spike Guard depend on. Juno's mobility kit complements Hazard's own aggressive positioning rather than working against it.
synergies.Junker Queen + Lúcio: Lúcio's healing aura offsets the damage Junker Queen takes trading Carnage swings at close range, letting her stay in the fight longer to keep wound damage ticking. Speed Song also helps her stick to a target she's already wounded rather than letting them create distance. Together they sustain an aggressive brawl neither could hold alone.
synergies.Junker Queen + Kiriko: Kiriko's Swift Step lets her follow Junker Queen straight into the middle of a Rampage charge, keeping her healed while she wounds and anti-heals the entire enemy team. Protection Suzu also cleanses crowd control that would otherwise interrupt her Carnage combo. Junker Queen's Adrenaline Rush and Kiriko's healing stack to keep her sustained through extended brawls.
synergies.Junker Queen + Reaper: Both heroes punish grouped enemies who can't be healed through, since Rampage's anti-heal effect locks down sustain right as Reaper's Death Blossom spins through the same cluster. Junker Queen's Commanding Shout also gives Reaper the temporary health to survive the exposed window Death Blossom requires. The pair overwhelms clustered fights that neither could break alone.
synergies.Junker Queen + Mei: Mei's Ice Wall can trap enemies in place for Junker Queen's Carnage or a thrown Jagged Blade, turning a normal engage into a guaranteed hit. Junker Queen's Commanding Shout speed boost also helps the pair close distance together before Mei needs to rely on her close-range beam. Both heroes benefit from forcing fights into tight, controlled spaces.
synergies.Mauga + Reaper: Cardiac Overdrive's damage reduction and life-steal-on-damage buff cover Reaper during Death Blossom's exposed spin, letting him stay alive through the burst that usually punishes the ultimate's lack of mobility. Both heroes want to be in the same tight, point-blank brawl, so their damage overlaps naturally instead of competing for space. Mauga's Overrun can also force enemies into the corner Death Blossom needs.
synergies.Orisa + Sojourn: Fortify and Energy Javelin control the space around Sojourn while she charges up a Railgun shot, protecting the vulnerable buildup her kit depends on. Terra Surge's pull can also group enemies together right as Sojourn's Overclock comes online, letting her burst down multiple targets in quick succession. Orisa's frontline presence buys Sojourn all the safety she needs to play for value.
synergies.Ramattra + Reaper: Ravenous Vortex traps grouped enemies in a slowing field right where Reaper's Death Blossom does the most damage, turning a normal team fight into a guaranteed multi-kill setup. Nemesis Form's frontline presence also gives Reaper the cover he needs to Shadow Step into position safely. The two heroes both want enemies clustered and unable to disengage, which makes their kits reinforce each other directly.
synergies.Sigma + Zenyatta: Experimental Barrier shields Zenyatta's fragile 50 health and 150 shields while he holds Orb of Discord on the enemy's biggest threat, letting Sigma's Hyperspheres and Zenyatta's own Orb of Destruction punish the amplified target together. Accretion's knockdown can also set up a clean elimination once the discord orb has stacked enough damage amplification. The pairing turns Zenyatta's usual fragility into a non-issue.
synergies.Sigma + Widowmaker: Experimental Barrier gives Widowmaker a safe sightline to snipe from without exposing her fragile 200 health to a flanker, and Sigma can reposition the barrier the instant her angle gets compromised. Kinetic Grasp also punishes anyone who dives toward her position with a projectile. It's a natural anchor-and-sniper pairing where each hero covers the other's biggest weakness.
synergies.D.Va + Echo: Both heroes bring independent aerial mobility that lets them combo pressure on the same target from different angles simultaneously. D.Va's Defense Matrix can screen Echo's Flight approach from incoming fire, and Echo's Focusing Beam finishes off targets D.Va has already worn down below half health. Their shared flexibility lets them coordinate dives without relying on ground positioning.
synergies.D.Va + Shion: D.Va's Defense Matrix can absorb the burst damage that punishes Shion for overextending during a flank, giving her the safety net she lacks on her own. Both heroes thrive on the same aggressive, close-range playstyle, so they naturally converge on the same isolated targets. Shion's Evade lets her capitalize on openings D.Va's dive creates before the enemy team can regroup.
synergies.D.Va + Sombra: Sombra's Hack strips away the defensive cooldowns, like a barrier or an escape ability, that would otherwise save a target from D.Va's dive, right as she commits Boosters into the fight. Her Translocator can also reposition to set up simultaneous engages with D.Va's mech. Between Defense Matrix and Stealth, the pair can approach almost any fight on their own terms.
synergies.Reinhardt + Symmetra: Symmetra's Teleporter lets the rest of the team flank around the choke point Reinhardt is holding with Barrier Field, opening up angles his shield alone can't create. Her Sentry Turrets also zone off flanking routes so his frontline focus isn't punished from the side. The pairing turns a static shield hold into active map control instead of a stalemate.
synergies.Sigma + Torbjörn: Experimental Barrier protects Torbjörn while he sets up Deploy Turret, covering the vulnerable window before it's active and dealing damage. Both heroes are built around holding a choke point with area denial, so their kits reinforce the same defensive position rather than competing for space. Sigma's Kinetic Grasp also absorbs incoming fire aimed at destroying the turret.
synergies.Orisa + Torbjörn: Fortify and Energy Javelin protect the sightline Torbjörn's turret needs to keep firing, denying enemies the burst damage that would normally destroy it quickly. Both heroes are built to anchor a single choke point rather than roam, so they reinforce each other's static, high-pressure hold. Orisa's armour also soaks up damage that would otherwise force Torbjörn to relocate his setup.
synergies.Junker Queen + Vendetta: Both are close-range duelists whose kits reward committing fully to a single target, so together they overwhelm a target faster than either could alone. Junker Queen's Commanding Shout gives Vendetta the temporary health and speed to stick to a duel through Warding Stance windows, while Vendetta's Onslaught stacks reward the same aggressive uptime Junker Queen's Adrenaline Rush wants. Neither hero needs much support to make this pairing work.
synergies.Doomfist + Venture: Both heroes are dive threats who can appear from unexpected angles, Doomfist via Rocket Punch and Seismic Slam, Venture via Burrow, putting a backline under pressure from two directions it can't track at once. Their overlapping mobility windows mean the enemy team often can't tell which flank to watch, splitting their attention and their support's healing. Together they create the kind of chaotic, simultaneous engagement neither could manufacture solo.
synergies.Orisa + Wuyang: Fortify buys Wuyang the safety to keep Restorative Stream active on frontline allies without being punished for standing still, while his Guardian Wave heals through the chip damage Orisa takes holding a choke. Terra Surge's pull can also group enemies right where Wuyang's Tidal Blast detonation does the most work. The pairing lets both heroes hold ground far longer than they could individually.
synergies.Sigma + Wuyang: Experimental Barrier protects Wuyang while he channels Restorative Stream on an ally, covering the exposed positioning his healing requires. In turn, Wuyang's Guardian Wave heals Sigma through the moments he's caught out repositioning his barrier. Both heroes benefit from controlled, protected space rather than open brawling, which keeps their playstyles well aligned.
synergies.Roadhog + Wuyang: Wuyang's Restorative Stream and Guardian Wave keep Roadhog topped up through the risky hook-and-shotgun trades his kit depends on, letting him play more aggressively than Take a Breather alone would allow. Guardian Wave's healing amplification also means a well-timed heal on Roadhog goes even further right after a big combo. The pairing turns Roadhog's usual all-or-nothing engages into much safer bets.
synergies.Bastion + Mauga: Mauga's Cardiac Overdrive reduces incoming damage and lets both heroes heal from damage dealt, giving Bastion the survivability to sit in Configuration: Assault far longer than he could alone, while his sustained minigun fire benefits from Overrun clearing the path to a good firing line. Together they create a wall of unrelenting sustained damage that's difficult for a team to safely peel from.
synergies.Cassidy + Orisa: Orisa's Fortify and Energy Javelin lock down space and create firing angles that let Cassidy line up a devastating Deadeye without worrying about a flank, and Terra Surge often clusters enemies into the perfect group for his ultimate to sweep through. In return, Peacekeeper can pick off anyone Javelin Spin exposes by clearing their cover.
synergies.Cassidy + Roadhog: Chain Hook pulls an isolated target directly into point-blank Peacekeeper range, turning a single hook into a near-guaranteed elimination, and Roadhog's health pool tanks damage while Cassidy lines up the follow-up shot. Flashbang can also stun the same target Roadhog just hooked, removing any chance of them fighting back before the kill.
synergies.Ashe + Sigma: Accretion knocks enemies down into a vulnerable state that gives Ashe a clean window to land precise Viper shots or detonate Dynamite on the exposed group, and Experimental Barrier gives her a safe firing position to peek from. B.O.B. also pairs well with Gravitic Flux, since enemies lifted and slammed together make an easy target for his arm cannon to finish off.
synergies.Genji + Winston: Winston's Jump Pack and Barrier Projector create the initial chaos and space for Swift Strike to dive backline targets safely, and both share the mobility to dive together and disengage together if the trade goes wrong. Primal Rage also creates the disruption Genji needs to land Dragonblade cleanly on a scattered team.
synergies.Genji + Wrecking Ball: Piledriver and Grappling Claw scatter a backline into disarray, and Swift Strike capitalizes on that chaos to pick off isolated targets before they can regroup. Adaptive Shield also soaks up retaliation while Genji resets Swift Strike off eliminations, letting both heroes chain pressure together.
synergies.Wrecking Ball + Zenyatta: Piledriver and Grappling Claw disrupt enemy formations from unusual angles, creating exactly the chaos that lets Zenyatta land Orb of Discord on a priority target before allies punish it. Orb of Harmony also keeps Wrecking Ball topped up during his aggressive engages, letting him stay in the fight longer than Adaptive Shield alone would allow.
synergies.Genji + Zarya: Graviton Surge clusters enemies together, and Dragonblade turns that cluster into a guaranteed multi-kill with repeated melee slashes. Projected Barrier can also be placed on Genji before he dives, letting him soak some punishment while charging Zarya's Particle Cannon in the process.
synergies.Anran + Winston: Jump Pack and Primal Rage create the frontline chaos that lets Inferno Rush and Dancing Blaze find isolated targets to burn down without immediate retaliation. Barrier Projector also gives Anran a safe space to duck into if Vermillion Revival isn't ready and she needs to reset.
synergies.Anran + D.Va: Defense Matrix can delete incoming burst damage aimed at Anran while she's mid-dash with Inferno Rush, and Boosters help create the same kind of chaotic engagement she thrives in. If Anran goes down without her ultimate ready, D.Va's Self-Destruct can still follow up on the same cluster of enemies she was pressuring.
synergies.Anran + Kiriko: Protection Suzu cleanses and grants brief invulnerability, which is exactly the safety net that lets Anran commit fully to Inferno Rush and Dancing Blaze without fear of crowd control locking her down mid-combo. Swift Step can also reposition Kiriko instantly to back her up the moment a dive goes wrong.
synergies.Anran + Lúcio: Speed Song lets Anran close distance even faster than Inferno Rush alone, letting her chain flanks before enemies can reposition, and Sound Barrier's overhealth gives her a buffer to survive the burst damage waiting on the other side. If the dive fails, Amp It Up healing helps her recover fast enough to try again.
synergies.Ashe + Mercy: Mercy's damage boost turns Ashe's already-precise Viper shots and Dynamite explosions into serious burst, especially when timed with a scoped headshot. In turn, B.O.B. creates a distraction that gives Mercy safe space to heal or boost from a distance without being an immediate target herself.
synergies.Ashe + Zenyatta: Orb of Discord marks a target for 30% more damage from all sources, which makes Ashe's already-precise Viper shots and Dynamite hit far harder, and Orb of Harmony keeps her topped up while she holds an angle. B.O.B. also gives Zenyatta a moment of breathing room to reposition safely.
synergies.Ashe + Baptiste: Amplification Matrix doubles the damage of any of Ashe's shots or Dynamite that pass through it, turning her already-precise damage into burst that can end a fight in seconds. Immortality Field also gives her the confidence to hold an aggressive angle without fear of a surprise pick.
synergies.Bastion + Mercy: Mercy's Caduceus Staff can either heal Bastion through Configuration: Assault's exposure or boost his already-massive damage output by 25%, both of which make his transformation window even more dangerous. Guardian Angel also lets her reposition to safety quickly if his stationary form draws unwanted attention her way.
